about.desc: We believe that travel is an art form. For over a decade, we have dedicated ourselves to curating the most extraordinary, bespoke journeys through the ancient landscapes of Egypt, Jordan, and Turkey. Our mission is to seamlessly blend world-class luxury with authentic cultural immersion.

blogs.A felucca relies entirely on the w: A felucca relies entirely on the wind and the river's current, meaning the journey dictates its own pace. You will recline on soft cushions laid across the deck, shielded from the Egyptian sun by a canvas canopy. As you drift silently past lush riverbanks, local fishermen casting their nets, and the timeless silhouettes of mud-brick villages, you gain a perspective of rural Egyptian life that is impossible to see from the deck of a large ship.

blogs.As you reach the end of the Siq, t: As you reach the end of the Siq, the narrow slit in the rocks suddenly opens to reveal The Treasury (Al-Khazneh), illuminated by over 1,500 candles spread across the sandy floor. The sight is nothing short of breathtaking. Visitors are invited to sit on rugs scattered across the ground, served traditional sweet Bedouin tea, and treated to a hauntingly beautiful performance of traditional music played on the Rababa (a stringed instrument) and the Ney (a wooden flute).
blogs.Beyond Karnak and the Valley of th: Beyond Karnak and the Valley of the Kings lie incredible, lesser-known temples waiting to be explored. Discover the secrets of ancient Thebes without the crowds.
blogs.Combining multiple countries into: Combining multiple countries into a single itinerary offers an unparalleled perspective on the region's history and culture. By traveling across borders, you can witness the transitions between ancient empires, compare culinary traditions, and experience the diverse landscapes that define the Middle East and Mediterranean. From the ancient pyramids of Egypt and the rose-red cliffs of Petra to the historic streets of Istanbul and the futuristic skyline of Dubai, a multi-country tour is the ultimate way to fulfill your travel dreams. Dunas Travel ensures a seamless journey, coordinating flights, private transfers, and handpicked local guides to make your multi-destination odyssey comfortable and stress-free.

blogs.Drifting silently over fairy chimn: Drifting silently over fairy chimneys at sunrise is a transcendent experience. Here is everything you need to know about booking a balloon flight in Turkey.
blogs.Dubai has successfully redefined t: Dubai has successfully redefined the boundaries of modern travel and architectural ambition. What was once a quiet fishing village has transformed into a global metropolis known for its record-breaking wonders. Visitors can stand on the observation deck of the Burj Khalifa, the tallest building in the world, shop at the sprawling Dubai Mall, or dine in Michelin-starred restaurants. Yet, amidst the futuristic skyscrapers, Dubai has preserved its cultural roots. A traditional wooden abra ride across Dubai Creek takes you to the historic gold and spice souks, where haggling is still an art form. The surrounding desert offers thrilling dune bashing, camel riding, and traditional dinners under the stars. Dubai represents the pinnacle of modern luxury, making it the perfect destination for travelers who want to experience the future of travel.
blogs.Egypt is a destination that transc: Egypt is a destination that transcends time. Standing before the Great Pyramid of Giza—the only surviving wonder of the ancient world—is a humbling experience that connects you directly to the dawn of civilization. Beyond the pyramids, Egypt offers a rich tapestry of history, from the temple complexes of Luxor and Karnak to the beautiful tombs of the Valley of the Kings. Traveling along the Nile on a luxury river cruise allows you to witness rural life that has remained unchanged for millennia, while the vibrant streets of Cairo offer an exciting mix of historic mosques, Coptic churches, and bustling bazaars. For those seeking relaxation, the pristine coral reefs and luxury resorts of Sharm El Sheikh and Hurghada provide world-class diving and seaside comfort. Egypt is not just a place to visit; it is a journey into the history of humanity itself.

blogs.Further off the traditional touris: Further off the traditional tourist trail lies the Ramesseum, the memorial temple of Ramses II. While partially ruined, the sheer scale of the fallen colossal statue of the pharaoh—which inspired Percy Bysshe Shelley's famous poem 'Ozymandias'—is awe-inspiring. The romantic melancholy of these ruins, set against the backdrop of the Theban hills, makes it an essential stop for history enthusiasts and photographers alike.
blogs.Greece is a sun-drenched paradise: Greece is a sun-drenched paradise where ancient ruins stand proud against the backdrop of the deep blue Aegean Sea. The "Lo Mejor de Grecia – 09 Días" vacation package offers a legendary 9 Días / 8 Noches itinerary that blends classical mythology with island luxury. From the iconic Acropolis in Athens to the caldera cliffs of Santorini and the cosmopolitan beach clubs of Mykonos, this tour is designed for travelers seeking the ultimate Mediterranean escape. Savor fresh seafood, watch world-famous sunsets, and wander through historic villages while enjoying premium accommodations and private transfers. Here is everything you need to know about this spectacular Greek odyssey.
blogs.Greece is the birthplace of Wester: Greece is the birthplace of Western democracy, philosophy, and classical art, making it a destination of profound cultural significance. The Acropolis of Athens stands as a monument to human achievement, while the historic ruins of Delphi and Olympia tell stories of ancient gods and athletes. But Greece is equally famous for its natural beauty. The Greek islands are legendary, characterized by whitewashed villages, blue-domed churches, and crystal-clear waters. Santorini offers breathtaking sunsets over its volcanic caldera, Mykonos is known for its vibrant nightlife and beautiful beaches, and Crete boasts rugged gorges and rich culinary traditions. Greece offers a relaxed Mediterranean lifestyle, warm hospitality, and a perfect balance of history and relaxation.

blogs.Jordan is a sanctuary of peace and: Jordan is a sanctuary of peace and history in the heart of the Middle East. Its crown jewel, the ancient Nabatean city of Petra, is a marvel of engineering carved directly into rose-red sandstone cliffs. Entering through the narrow, towering canyon of the Siq to reveal the Treasury is a moment that stays with you forever. But Jordan is much more than Petra. The mineral-rich waters of the Dead Sea offer a unique wellness experience where you float effortlessly in a natural spa. Wadi Rum, the Valley of the Moon, presents a surreal landscape of red sand dunes and dramatic rock formations best explored in a 4x4 or spent under the stars in a luxury desert camp. With Roman ruins in Jerash, biblical sites like Mount Nebo, and warm Bedouin hospitality, Jordan is an enriching destination that offers adventure, history, and rejuvenation in equal measure.

blogs.Luxor, often described as the worl: Luxor, often described as the world's greatest open-air museum, is undeniably famous for the colossal Karnak Temple and the pharaohs' tombs in the Valley of the Kings. However, for the discerning traveler, the true magic of this ancient city lies off the beaten path, hidden amongst the sugar cane fields and lesser-visited desert valleys. These secondary temples offer a profound, uncrowded connection to Egypt's majestic past.
blogs.Luxury glamping has transformed th: Luxury glamping has transformed the Wadi Rum experience. You no longer need to sacrifice comfort to immerse yourself in the wilderness. High-end camps offer geodesic dome tents—often referred to as 'Martian Domes'—featuring transparent panoramic panels. From the comfort of a king-sized bed adorned with premium linens, you can watch the sun set the desert ablaze in reds and purples before giving way to the darkest, most star-filled sky you will ever witness.
blogs.Mornings in Wadi Rum are equally m: Mornings in Wadi Rum are equally majestic. Waking up early to take a silent camel ride into the desert as the sun rises, casting long, dramatic shadows across the canyons, is an unforgettable experience. Whether you spend your days hiking through narrow gorges, scrambling up rock bridges, or simply meditating in the vast emptiness, camping in Wadi Rum connects you to nature in its most raw and beautiful form.
blogs.Morocco is a sensory masterpiece,: Morocco is a sensory masterpiece, a land where vibrant colors, rich aromas, and historic architecture come together to create an unforgettable travel experience. The "Spices of Morocco" tour is a carefully crafted 05 Days / 04 Nights journey that guides you through imperial cities, historic medinas, and breathtaking landscapes. From the bustling market squares of Marrakech and the medieval alleys of Fez to the coastal allure of Casablanca, this itinerary is designed to show you the soul of the Maghreb. Dive into this guide to discover the highlights of our Moroccan program and learn practical tips to make your journey through this North African kingdom truly seamless.
blogs.Morocco is an exotic gateway to No: Morocco is an exotic gateway to North Africa, offering a rich blend of Arab, Berber, and European influences. The country's imperial cities—Marrakech, Fez, Rabat, and Meknes—are famous for their historic medinas, where maze-like alleys are filled with stalls selling colorful spices, hand-woven carpets, and traditional lanterns. The vibrant square of Jemaa el-Fna in Marrakech is a theater of street performers, musicians, and food stalls that comes alive at sunset. Beyond the cities, Morocco boasts the dramatic peaks of the Atlas Mountains, the vast dunes of the Sahara Desert, and beautiful coastal towns like Essaouira and Casablanca. Morocco is a feast for the senses, offering history, culture, and adventure around every corner.
blogs.Morocco's climate varies, making s: Morocco's climate varies, making spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) the best times to travel. Medford medinas can be crowded and labyrinthine, so hiring a local guide is highly recommended. Wear comfortable closed-toe walking shoes to navigate the narrow alleys. Dress respectfully by covering your shoulders and knees, especially in religious and rural areas. Haggling is a central part of shopping in the souks, so take your time and negotiate politely. Bring a jacket for cool evenings in the Atlas Mountains or the Sahara Desert. Carry local dirhams for small purchases and tips.
blogs.Most travelers opt for a short sun: Most travelers opt for a short sunset sail in Aswan or Luxor, which is a magical experience as the sky turns brilliant shades of gold and crimson. However, for the truly adventurous, multi-day felucca journeys are available. These trips involve sleeping on the deck under a blanket of stars and eating simple, delicious meals prepared by your Nubian crew over a small stove. It is a rustic experience, but one that offers unparalleled serenity.
blogs.Navigate the labyrinthine alleys o: Navigate the labyrinthine alleys of one of the world's oldest and largest covered markets. From Turkish delight to antique kilims, here is your ultimate shopping guide.
blogs.Navigating the bazaar can feel ove: Navigating the bazaar can feel overwhelming to the uninitiated. The key is to embrace the art of getting lost. The market is roughly organized by trade—one alley shimmers with gold jewelry, another is draped in vibrant textiles and carpets, while others are filled with the rich aromas of leather goods or the clinking of hand-painted ceramics. Allow your senses to guide you, and do not be afraid to wander off the main thoroughfares into the quieter, more specialized courtyards (han).
blogs.No culinary tour of Turkey is comp: No culinary tour of Turkey is complete without indulging its sweet tooth. While Baklava—layers of paper-thin phyllo dough filled with crushed pistachios and drenched in syrup—is world-famous, you must also try Künefe. This incredible dessert consists of a mild, stretchy cheese encased in shredded kadayıf dough, baked until crispy, soaked in sweet syrup, and served hot so the cheese pulls in glorious strings. It is the perfect conclusion to a Turkish feast.
blogs.One such marvel is the Temple of M: One such marvel is the Temple of Medinet Habu. Second only to Karnak in size, this magnificent mortuary temple of Ramses III is remarkably well-preserved. Its deeply carved hieroglyphs retain much of their original vibrant coloring, providing a vivid glimpse into ancient artistry. The towering pylons and massive courtyards are often devoid of the large tour groups, allowing visitors to wander in serene contemplation.
blogs.Planning your dream trip to the Py: Planning your dream trip to the Pyramids? Weather plays a crucial role. Read our comprehensive month-by-month guide to choosing the perfect season.
blogs.Shopping here is as much about the: Shopping here is as much about the social interaction as it is about the transaction. Haggling is expected and should be approached with a smile and a sense of humor. When a shopkeeper invites you in for a glass of apple tea (elma çayı), accept it! This hospitality is a cornerstone of Turkish commerce. Sit, sip, chat about your travels, and then begin the friendly negotiation. It is a dance that locals have perfected over centuries.
blogs.Sitting at 430 meters below sea le: Sitting at 430 meters below sea level, the Dead Sea is the lowest point on the surface of the Earth. Bordering Jordan to the east, this landlocked body of water is one of the world's most unique natural phenomena and has been revered as a sanctuary of health and healing since the days of King Herod and Cleopatra. Today, it remains the ultimate destination for wellness-focused DUNAS TRAVELers.
blogs.Spanning two continents, Turkey is: Spanning two continents, Turkey is a vibrant mosaic of Eastern and Western cultures, ancient history, and breathtaking landscapes. The "Admirable Turkey Winter - 10 Days / 9 Nights" itinerary is a premier 10 Days / 9 Nights journey that showcases the very best this diverse country has to offer. From the minarets and bustling bazaars of historic Istanbul to the fairy chimneys of Cappadocia and the thermal travertines of Pamukkale, this package is designed to show you Turkey in style. Whether you are cruising the Bosphorus, flying high in a hot air balloon at sunrise, or walking the marble streets of ancient Ephesus, this trip is packed with bucket-list moments. Let us explore why Turkey should be your next destination and how this tour makes it unforgettable.

blogs.The 'Low Season' spans the blazing: The 'Low Season' spans the blazing summer months from May to September. Temperatures in Upper Egypt (Luxor and Aswan) can regularly exceed 40°C (104°F), making midday sightseeing incredibly difficult. However, if you are willing to start your tours at dawn and spend your afternoons relaxing in a 5-star hotel pool or escaping to the luxurious Red Sea resorts of Sharm El-Sheikh and Hurghada, you will be rewarded with virtually empty temples and significant discounts on premium accommodations.
blogs.The 'Peak Season' occurs during th: The 'Peak Season' occurs during the winter months of December, January, and February. During this time, the weather is incredibly pleasant, with daytime temperatures in Cairo and Luxor hovering around a comfortable 20°C to 25°C (68°F to 77°F). This is the absolute best weather for exploring the Valley of the Kings or wandering around the Giza plateau without suffering from heat exhaustion. However, because the weather is ideal, this is also when the crowds are thickest and luxury hotel rates are at their premium.
blogs.The 'Shoulder Seasons' of October/: The 'Shoulder Seasons' of October/November and March/April are often considered the sweet spots. The weather is warm but not yet oppressive, and the massive winter crowds have either not yet arrived or have begun to disperse. This is an excellent time for a Nile Cruise, as you can comfortably lounge on the sun deck during the day and enjoy cool, breezy evenings. Note that late March and April can sometimes bring the 'Khamsin', a dry, dusty desert wind, but it is usually brief.
blogs.The Grand Bazaar (Kapalıçarşı) in: The Grand Bazaar (Kapalıçarşı) in Istanbul is not merely a market; it is a chaotic, colorful, and sensory labyrinth that has pulsed as the commercial heart of the city for over half a millennium. Spanning 61 covered streets and housing over 4,000 shops, it is one of the largest and oldest covered markets in the world. Stepping through its grand archways is like stepping into an Ottoman era time capsule.
blogs.The Siq, a narrow 1.2-kilometer go: The Siq, a narrow 1.2-kilometer gorge flanked by towering 80-meter-high sandstone cliffs, is lit only by the soft, flickering glow of paper lanterns lining the path. Walking through this winding canyon in near silence, accompanied only by the whisper of the wind and the crunch of gravel beneath your feet, builds an incredible sense of anticipation. It is a meditative walk that prepares you for the grand reveal.

blogs.The best time to visit Dubai is fr: The best time to visit Dubai is from November to March, when the weather is warm and pleasant, avoiding the extreme heat of the summer months. Dubai is a very modern city, but it is still important to respect Islamic culture; dress modestly in public areas such as malls and traditional souks. Pack lightweight summer clothes, but bring a light jacket or sweater for air-conditioned indoor spaces and cool desert safari nights. If you plan to visit the Burj Khalifa observation deck or fine-dining restaurants, pack smart-casual or formal wear. Taxis are clean and inexpensive, and credit cards are widely accepted everywhere.
blogs.The day inevitably begins with the: The day inevitably begins with the legendary Turkish Breakfast (Kahvaltı). This is not merely a meal, but a lavish morning ritual. A traditional kahvaltı spread covers the entire table with small plates: a variety of cheeses (like salty feta and mild kashkaval), black and green olives, fresh tomatoes, cucumbers, thick clotted cream (kaymak) drenched in local honey, spicy sujuk (sausage), and endless glasses of black tea, all accompanied by freshly baked simit—a sesame-encrusted bread ring.
blogs.The evening experience in a Bedoui: The evening experience in a Bedouin camp is rich with culture. Traditional Zarb—a meal of meat and vegetables slow-cooked in an underground sand oven—is unearthed and served communally. Following dinner, guests gather around a crackling campfire, sipping sweet sage tea, listening to ancient Bedouin folklore, and staring up at the Milky Way. The silence of the desert at night is profound, offering a rare and deep sense of isolation and peace.
blogs.The flight itself is incredibly pe: The flight itself is incredibly peaceful. Unlike a helicopter or airplane, a hot air balloon moves with the wind, resulting in a silent, motionless sensation of floating. As the sun begins to rise over the horizon, painting the sky in hues of peach, gold, and lavender, the full majesty of Cappadocia is revealed. Expert pilots skillfully navigate the balloons, sometimes rising thousands of feet for panoramic views, and other times dipping incredibly low, drifting just meters above the fruit orchards and weaving between the fairy chimneys.
blogs.The ideal months to visit Jordan a: The ideal months to visit Jordan are during the spring (March to May) or autumn (September to November) when temperatures are moderate. If you plan to float in the Dead Sea, remember to pack swimwear, water shoes to protect your feet from salt crystals, and avoid shaving beforehand to prevent stinging. Wadi Rum deserts can get surprisingly cold at night, so bring warm layers even in summer. Sturdy hiking shoes are highly recommended for walking the cobblestone trails of Petra. Respect local customs by dressing modestly, and always ask for permission before taking photos of local Bedouins. Drink bottled water and keep cash on hand for souvenirs.
blogs.The image of hundreds of colorful: The image of hundreds of colorful hot air balloons ascending simultaneously over the surreal, lunar-like landscape of Cappadocia is one of travel's most iconic visuals. This central Turkish region, famous for its 'fairy chimneys' (tall, cone-shaped rock formations), ancient cave dwellings, and underground cities, is arguably the best place in the world for hot air ballooning. For the DUNAS TRAVELer, this is an absolute, non-negotiable bucket list experience.
blogs.The magic begins before dawn. You: The magic begins before dawn. You are picked up from your luxury cave hotel while the stars are still out and driven to the launch site. As you sip hot coffee in the crisp morning air, you watch the massive nylon envelopes inflate, glowing like giant lightbulbs against the dark landscape as the burners roar to life. The anticipation builds until you step into the woven wicker basket and gently, almost imperceptibly, leave the earth behind.
blogs.The most famous aspect of the Dead: The most famous aspect of the Dead Sea is, of course, the buoyancy. The water is nearly ten times saltier than the ocean. This extreme hypersalinity means that swimming is practically impossible; instead, you effortlessly bob on the surface like a cork. Reading a newspaper or a book while floating on your back is the quintessential Dead Sea photo opportunity. However, it is the composition of the water, rich in magnesium, calcium, potassium, and bromide, that provides remarkable therapeutic benefits for the skin and joints.
blogs.The ritual of the Dead Sea experie: The ritual of the Dead Sea experience involves more than just the water. The shores are lined with dark, mineral-rich mud. Visitors are encouraged to scoop up this thick, velvety mud and slather it entirely over their bodies. After letting it bake and dry in the Jordanian sun—which is safer here due to the extra atmospheric layer filtering out harmful UV rays—you wade back into the sea to wash it off. The result is incredibly soft, exfoliated, and glowing skin.
blogs.There are few travel experiences a: There are few travel experiences as universally acclaimed yet deeply moving as Petra by Night. Operating only three times a week—Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays—this after-dark excursion transforms the already mystical ancient Nabatean city into a scene straight out of a desert fairy tale. The journey begins at the visitor center, but the true magic starts as you step into the Siq.
blogs.Timing is everything when planning: Timing is everything when planning a journey to Egypt. With a climate defined by scorching desert summers and mild, pleasant winters, choosing the right month can drastically alter your experience at open-air historical sites. The general consensus is that the prime tourist season runs from October to April, but each season offers its own unique advantages and challenges for the DUNAS TRAVELer.
blogs.To enjoy the Greek islands without: To enjoy the Greek islands without the intense summer heat and crowds, aim to visit in May-June or September-October. If you are traveling in July and August, pack lightweight resort wear, swimwear, a hat, and plenty of sunscreen. Comfortable sandals or walking shoes are essential for walking up the steps of the Acropolis or navigating the steep pathways of Santorini's villages. Savoring local taverna dishes is a highlight, and tipping is appreciated but not mandatory. Credit cards are widely accepted, but having a few Euros cash is helpful for small island shops.
blogs.To ensure a comfortable journey th: To ensure a comfortable journey through Egypt, it is best to plan your visit between October and April, when the weather is cooler and perfect for outdoor sightseeing. Pack lightweight, breathable cotton clothing to stay cool, a wide-brimmed hat, sunglasses, and high-SPF sunscreen to protect against the desert sun. Sturdy, comfortable walking shoes are essential for exploring temple ruins and dusty archaeological sites. While beach resorts are very relaxed, it is recommended to dress modestly when exploring historic Cairo or local towns (covering shoulders and knees). Always drink bottled water, carry some local currency (Egyptian Pounds) for small tips, and keep your camera charged for the incredible sights.
blogs.To ensure a premium experience, it: To ensure a premium experience, it is crucial to book a 'Comfort' or 'VIP' flight. Standard baskets can hold up to 28 people, which can feel crowded. Luxury options limit the basket to 8-12 passengers, providing ample space to move around and take unobstructed photographs. Flights are highly dependent on weather and wind conditions, so it is highly recommended to book your flight for your very first morning in Cappadocia, allowing backup days in case of cancellation.
blogs.To make the most of this experienc: To make the most of this experience, arrive early to secure a good spot, and remember to respect the silence of the canyon. Photography can be challenging in the extreme low light; bring a tripod if you wish to capture the scene, but also remember to put the camera down and simply absorb the atmosphere. Petra by Night is not just a visual spectacle; it is a spiritual journey back in time.
blogs.To maximize your experience, book: To maximize your experience, book a stay at one of the luxury resorts that line the Jordanian coast. These properties offer private beach access, freshwater pools (essential after the intense sting of the salt water), and world-class spas that utilize the local minerals in their high-end treatments. The Dead Sea is not just a geological oddity; it is a restorative retreat for the mind, body, and soul.
blogs.Towering sandstone mountains, crim: Towering sandstone mountains, crimson sands, and a sky exploding with stars. Discover why camping in Jordan's Valley of the Moon is the ultimate desert escape.
blogs.Tunisia is a hidden gem on the Med: Tunisia is a hidden gem on the Mediterranean, boasting a history that dates back to the Phoenicians and Romans. The ancient ruins of Carthage, once a powerful rival to Rome, and the spectacular Roman amphitheater of El Jem are UNESCO World Heritage sites that rival any in Europe. The picturesque blue-and-white village of Sidi Bou Said offers winding cobblestone streets and sea views, while the holy city of Kairouan is home to the oldest mosque in North Africa. Tunisia also features beautiful sandy beaches, olive groves, and the vast dunes of the Sahara Desert, which served as the backdrop for the Star Wars movies. It is a destination of incredible depth, offering history, culture, and natural beauty without the crowds.
blogs.Tunisia is best visited between Ma: Tunisia is best visited between March and June, or September and November, avoiding the hot summer months. Pack comfortable clothing suitable for walking around Roman ruins and sandy desert dunes. Dress modestly when visiting mosques or traditional markets. Sunscreen, a hat, and sunglasses are highly recommended. Tap water is not recommended for drinking, so stick to bottled water. Bring local Tunisian Dinars, as cash is preferred in smaller towns and traditional souks.
blogs.Tunisia, the jewel of North Africa: Tunisia, the jewel of North Africa, is a destination rich in ancient history, Mediterranean charm, and diverse landscapes. The "Tunisia 8 Days Desert & Sea" itinerary is a magnificent tunisia_tour_duration travel program that takes you from the historic ruins of Carthage and the blue-washed streets of Sidi Bou Said to the golden beaches of the coast and the vast expanse of the Sahara. Dunas Travel has meticulously designed this tour to offer a balanced blend of cultural exploration and premium relaxation. In this article, we highlight the unique attractions included in this tour and provide practical tips to prepare you for an unforgettable Tunisian adventure.
blogs.Turkey is a land where continents: Turkey is a land where continents collide and civilizations blend. As the bridge between Europe and Asia, it has been home to the Byzantine, Roman, and Ottoman empires, leaving behind a wealth of historical sites. Istanbul, a city split by the Bosphorus, offers iconic landmarks like Hagia Sophia, the Blue Mosque, and the labyrinthine Grand Bazaar. In Cappadocia, the landscape transforms into a fantasy world of fairy chimneys, ancient cave churches, and underground cities, made even more magical when viewed from a hot air balloon at sunrise. Along the Aegean and Mediterranean coasts, you will find pristine turquoise waters, sandy beaches, and ancient ruins like Ephesus and Troy. Turkey's rich culinary tradition, thermal spas in Pamukkale, and historic monuments make it an incredibly diverse and rewarding destination for any traveler.
blogs.Turkey is best visited in the spri: Turkey is best visited in the spring (April to May) or autumn (September to October) when the weather is mild and crowds are manageable. When visiting historic mosques such as Hagia Sophia or the Blue Mosque, women are required to cover their heads with a scarf, and both men and women must cover their shoulders and knees. Pack comfortable walking shoes for the cobblestone streets of Istanbul and the ruins of Ephesus. If you plan a hot air balloon flight in Cappadocia, bring a warm jacket, as early mornings in the balloon basket can be chilly. It is common to negotiate prices in the Grand Bazaar, so approach it with a friendly smile.
blogs.Turkish cuisine is a magnificent t: Turkish cuisine is a magnificent tapestry woven from the historical influences of the Ottoman Empire, bridging Central Asian traditions with Mediterranean and Middle Eastern flavors. It is a culinary culture that reveres fresh ingredients, masterful spicing, and the joy of communal dining. A journey to Turkey is as much an exploration of taste as it is of history, and missing out on its authentic dishes is missing out on the country's soul.
blogs.Turkish food is a rich fusion of C: Turkish food is a rich fusion of Central Asian, Middle Eastern, and Mediterranean flavors. Here are the iconic dishes you absolutely must taste during your visit.

blogs.Wadi Rum, known as the Valley of t: Wadi Rum, known as the Valley of the Moon, is a landscape of such alien beauty that it has served as the backdrop for countless science fiction films. Located in southern Jordan, this vast expanse of crimson sand and towering, monolith-like sandstone mountains offers one of the most spectacular desert environments on earth. However, a daytime jeep tour only scratches the surface; to truly understand Wadi Rum, you must spend the night.
blogs.When booking a felucca, especially: When booking a felucca, especially for overnight trips, ensure you go through a reputable DUNAS TRAVEL concierge to guarantee a well-maintained boat and a highly experienced captain. While you forfeit air conditioning and private bathrooms, the reward is an intimate, eco-friendly, and profoundly peaceful immersion into the very soul of Egypt.
blogs.When embarking on a multi-destinat: When embarking on a multi-destination tour, ensure your passport has at least six months of validity and several empty pages for visas. Research the visa requirements for each country in advance, as some can be obtained online or upon arrival. Pack versatile clothing that can be layered to suit different climates, from breezy coastal cities to cool desert nights. Sturdy walking shoes, sunscreen, and adapters for different plug types are essential. Carry a mix of credit cards and local currency for each country, and always keep digital copies of your travel documents.
blogs.When it comes to main courses, the: When it comes to main courses, the kebab reigns supreme, but the variety goes far beyond the familiar döner. The Adana Kebab, named after the southern city, is a masterpiece of spicy minced lamb grilled on a wide skewer over charcoal. For something richer, the İskender Kebab from Bursa features thin slices of grilled lamb basted with hot tomato sauce over pieces of pita bread, generously slathered with melted sheep's milk butter and served with yogurt.
blogs.When looking for authentic souveni: When looking for authentic souvenirs, seek out high-quality Turkish towels (peshtemals), intricate Iznik ceramics, or a genuine hand-woven kilim rug. Be wary of cheap imports and always ask the vendor about the origin and craftsmanship of their wares. Whether you leave with an antique brass lantern or simply a bag of freshly dusted Turkish delight, the true treasure of the Grand Bazaar is the unforgettable experience of the marketplace itself.
blogs.While multi-story luxury cruise sh: While multi-story luxury cruise ships offer undeniable comfort, there is no more authentic or peaceful way to experience the Nile River than aboard a traditional felucca. These simple, wooden sailing boats, distinguished by their lateen (triangular) sails, have navigated the waters of the Nile since the time of the pharaohs. A felucca trip strips away the noise and haste of modern travel, replacing it with the gentle lap of water and the whispering of the wind.

classic.day1.body: On the first day, we will be waiting for you at Cairo airport to give you your visa, help you with immigration checks and take you to your hotel.
classic.day1.title: City of Origin – Cairo
classic.day2.body: After breakfast at the hotel, departure to visit the Pyramids of Giza: Cheops, Chephren and Mycerinus. Then, stop at the panoramic viewpoint of the pyramids for beautiful photos and then visit the Sphinx, head of the Pharaoh and body of the Lion, guardian of the Pyramids. Optional visit to Memphis and Saqqara, the ruins of the original Cairo.
classic.day2.title: Cairo
classic.day3.body: After eating we will head to the airport to take a flight to Aswan, we will pick you up at Aswan airport. Then we will take you to the cruise ship, where full board accommodation awaits you. We will visit the Temple of Philae: the perfect beginning!
classic.day3.title: Cairo / Aswan
classic.day4.body: After breakfast, optional visit to the temples of Abu Simbel, which is very much appreciated and recommended. Then, back on the boat, we will eat and begin to sail along the Nile, arriving at Kom Ombo. Then, after visiting the temple, we will continue sailing towards Edfu, where we will anchor for dinner and overnight stay.
classic.day4.title: Nile Cruise (full board)
classic.day5.body: After eating, we will visit the Temple of Edfu, dedicated to the god Horus. The cruise will then continue towards Luxor. Upon arriving in the majestic city of Luxor we will visit two of the most spectacular temples in the country, the temples of Luxor and Karnak. These temples were separated in antiquity by the Avenue of the Sphinxes, 3 kilometres long. We will eat and spend the night anchored in Luxor.
classic.day5.title: Nile Cruise (full board)
classic.day6.body: Breakfast at the hotel. At the agreed time, transfer to Hurghada airport to take the domestic flight to Cairo. Our assistance and transfer to hotel.
classic.day6.title: Luxor – Cairo
classic.day7.body: Breakfast and free day. An optional scheduled excursion is planned to the most emblematic places in the city centre. We will see the Citadel, visit the Alabaster Mosque, explore Khan el Khalili, cross the Coptic Quarter, have lunch and admire the masterpieces of the Egyptian Museum. Then in the evening we will have dinner at a local restaurant.
classic.day7.title: Cairo
classic.day8.body: After breakfast we will check out of the rooms, take you to the airport and assist you in taking your international flight.
classic.day8.title: Cairo – City of Origin
classic.excluded.1: General travel tips 45€ per adult or minor (guide tips not included).
classic.excluded.2: Meals, drinks or any other expense not mentioned as included.
classic.highlight.1: Visit to the iconic Pyramids of Giza and the Sphinx
classic.highlight.2: Nile cruise with full board from Aswan to Luxor
classic.highlight.3: Temples of Philae, Kom Ombo, Edfu, Karnak and Luxor
classic.highlight.5: Exploration of historic Cairo: the Citadel, the Alabaster Mosque, Khan el Khalili, the Coptic Quarter and the Egyptian Museum
classic.included.1: All transport from start to finish.
classic.included.4: Visits, tickets and transfers according to the itinerary.
classic.included.5: 04 nights in Cairo bed and breakfast plus 03 nights Nile cruise full board.
classic.overview: An unforgettable journey through Egypt's millennia-old wonders. From the mystery of the Pyramids of Giza to the magic of the Nile, from the tombs of the Pharaohs to the vibrant atmosphere of historic Cairo — this classic programme is designed for those who want to experience Egypt in a complete, comfortable and authentic way.
classic.shortDesc: An unforgettable journey through Egypt's millennia-old wonders. From the mystery of the Pyramids of Giza to the magic of the Nile, from the tombs of the Pharaohs to the vibrant atmosphere of historic Cairo — this classic programme is designed for those who want to experience Egypt in a complete, comfortable and authentic way.
classic.title: Classic Egypt Programme

data.A Pérola do Mediterrâneo: Alexandri: The Pearl of the Mediterranean: Alexandria
data.A bordo: orquesta, bailarines de da: On board: orchestra, Greek dancers and Greek food included.
data.A bordo: orquesta, cantantes, baila: On board: orchestra, singers, traditional Greek dancers, and delicious Greek food included.
data.A comprehensive luxury journey cove: A comprehensive luxury journey covering Amman, Petra, Wadi Rum, Aqaba, and the Dead Sea.
data.A deeply immersive historical tour: A deeply immersive historical tour focusing on the monumental achievements of the Pharaohs in Cairo and Luxor.
data.A felucca relies entirely on the wi: A felucca relies entirely on the wind and the river's current, meaning the journey dictates its own pace. You will recline on soft cushions laid across the deck, shielded from the Egyptian sun by a canvas canopy. As you drift silently past lush riverbanks, local fishermen casting their nets, and the timeless silhouettes of mud-brick villages, you gain a perspective of rural Egyptian life that is impossible to see from the deck of a large ship.
data.A gastronomic journey through Istan: A gastronomic journey through Istanbul, tasting authentic street food, fine dining, and taking private cooking classes.
data.A high-altitude adventure camping n: A high-altitude adventure camping near the ancient, massive stone heads of King Antiochus to catch a legendary sunrise.
data.A jornada definitiva pelo Egito dos: The definitive journey through the Egypt of the pharaohs. Sail the legendary Nile between Aswan and Luxor aboard an elegant, high-end cruise, discovering the secrets of the Philae Temple, the colossi of Karnak and the magic of the Valley of the Kings — combining navigation with the Grand Egyptian Museum in Cairo.
data.A las 15:00 h: emocionante safari 4: At 3:00 p.m.: Exciting 4×4 safari in the Dubai desert. Dunes, photos at golden sunset.
data.A las 15:00 h: safari 4×4 en el des: At 3:00 p.m.: 4×4 safari in the Dubai desert. Gliding over golden dunes and photos at sunset.
At 3:00 p.m.: 4×4 safari in the desert. Golden sand dunes, sunset photos.
data.A lavish Babylonian-inspired resort: A lavish Babylonian-inspired resort offering floating infinity pools and the largest spa in the Middle East.
data.A luxurious beach holiday on the Tu: A luxurious beach holiday on the Turquoise Coast, combined with visits to ancient Lycian ruins.
data.A luxury Nile River Cruise is the q: A luxury Nile River Cruise is the quintessential Egyptian experience, combining the relaxation of a 5-star resort with the adventure of an archaeological expedition. Sailing between Luxor and Aswan (usually over 4 to 5 days), these multi-deck ships serve as your floating luxury hotel, allowing you to unpack just once while the wonders of ancient Egypt glide past your panoramic cabin window.
data.A premium 14-day spiritual and hist: A premium 14-day spiritual and historical journey tracing Coptic monasteries in Egypt and the ancient wonders of Jordan.
data.A thrilling off-road jeep adventure: A thrilling off-road jeep adventure through the rugged mountains of Sinai, complete with a traditional Bedouin dinner.
data.A união definitiva entre o fascínio: The definitive union between the fascination of antiquity and natural beauty in an eleven-day itinerary. Travel through time in Cairo and sail down the River Nile discovering the secrets of the pharaohs in Luxor and Aswan. The perfect climax reserves three nights in Sharm El Sheikh, an authentic underwater paradise with rich marine life and coral reefs, all accompanied by high standard all-inclusive service.
data.AMMAN / MADABA / MOUNT NEBO / SHOBA: AMMAN / MADABA / MOUNT NEBO / SHOBAK PANORAMIC / PETRA
data.Abarca la grandeza de tres civiliza: It encompasses the grandeur of three majestic civilizations in an epic 14-day journey through Egypt, Greece and Türkiye. Start with the pharaonic legacies in Cairo and the Nile, then ascend to the Acropolis of Athens and cross the Greek islands. End this exceptional odyssey in enigmatic Istanbul, getting lost among Ottoman palaces, legendary bazaars and the iconic Bosphorus.
data.Abu Dhabi City Tour (guía en españo: Abu Dhabi City Tour (guide in Spanish, shared)
data.Abu Dhabi: Mezquita Sheikh Zayed y: Abu Dhabi: Sheikh Zayed Mosque and Louvre
data.Access to pool and fitness center: Access to pool and fitness center
data.Accoglienza all'aeroporto, sbrigo v: Reception at the airport, visa processing and transfer to the hotel.
data.Accoglienza personalizzata all'aero: Personalized welcome at the airport, visa included.
data.Accommodations blend opulent Ottoma: Accommodations blend opulent Ottoman heritage with contemporary luxury. The suites feature high ceilings, antique-style furnishings, and lavish marble bathrooms. Waking up and stepping out onto a private balcony to watch the ferries glide up and down the sparkling Bosphorus is an experience exclusive to this magnificent property.
data.Acolhimento especial no aeroporto i: Special welcome at the international airport.
data.Acomodação nas cabines do cruzeiro: Accommodation in the cruise cabins and lunch on board.
data.Acomodação no Cruzeiro e almoço a b: Accommodation on the Cruise and lunch on board.
data.Acrópolis de Atenas y el Partenón: Acropolis of Athens and the Parthenon
data.After breakfast at the hotel, depar: After breakfast at the hotel, departure for Alexandria (598km). Stop in Marsa Matrouh for lunch at a local restaurant (excluding drinks). Afterwards, continue to Alexandria and check into the hotel. Dinner included (excluding drinks) and overnight stay.
data.After breakfast at the hotel, early: After breakfast at the hotel, early departure towards the Wadi El Natroun Valley, named after the 8 different lakes in the region where natron salt is produced. Visit to the Monastery of Anba Bishoy (116KM), the most famous monastery of the Coptic Orthodox Church of Alexandria. Continue to El Alamein (176KM) on the shores of the Mediterranean, stop for lunch at a local restaurant (excluding drinks). In the afternoon, we will visit the WWII Museum and the Commonwealth Tombs, continuing along the Mediterranean coast to Marsa Matrouh (184KM). Arrival at the Marsa Matrouh Hotel. Dinner (excluding drinks) and overnight stay.
data.After breakfast at the hotel, we wi: After breakfast at the hotel, we will visit the ancient capital of Egypt for over 1,000 years, famous for the Lighthouse of Alexandria (one of the 7 wonders of the ancient world). We will visit the Kom El Shoqafa catacombs, Pompey's Pillar (a monument carved from a single piece of red granite weighing 396 tons), Montazah Palace, and the Qaitbay Citadel, built on the exact site of the former lighthouse. We will also visit the famous Library of Alexandria (closed on Fridays), one of the largest and most important in the world. Stop for an included lunch at a local restaurant (excluding drinks). Next, we return to Cairo (218 km) directly to the airport or your chosen hotel. End of services.
data.After breakfast, check out from the: After breakfast, check out from the hotel and visit Rommel's Cave, featuring rock paintings dating back more than 5,000 years. Continue to Siwa Oasis (307KM) crossing the Sahara Desert. Arrival and accommodation at the Siwa Shali Resort Hotel, where we will have some time to rest. Stop for lunch at a local restaurant (excluding drinks). Late afternoon, a walk through the Shali Old Village to contemplate the sunset. Return to the hotel, dinner (excluding drinks), and overnight stay.
data.After breakfast, we will visit the: After breakfast, we will visit the Mountain of the Dead, the Temple of Amun, and Cleopatra's Baths. Stop for lunch (excluding drinks). In the afternoon, we will head out in a 4x4 vehicle to the Great Sand Sea, arriving at the desert tents. Dinner (excluding drinks) and overnight stay in the camp.
data.Afternoon visit to Al Adra Al Adawi: Afternoon visit to Al Adra Al Adawia Church (El Maadi) and the Monastery of Saint Simon (Cave Church) carved in the Mokattam mountain.

Time for craft workshops of onyx and semi-precious stones. In the evening, optional excursion to a show of traditional dances. Dinner and accommodation. Meals: Breakfast and dinner.

We will then leave to visit the Library of Alexandria (one of the oldest in the world), which was built on the exact spot where the Lighthouse of Alexandria used to stand. data.As the afternoon heat begins to wan: As the afternoon heat begins to wane, the safari stops at strategic viewpoints offering breathtaking panoramas of the desert landscape. Guests have the opportunity to hike through narrow, colorful slot canyons—often referred to as the 'Colored Canyon'—where the rock walls swirl with vibrant hues of red, yellow, and purple sandstone.
data.As you cruise away from the sandy s: As you cruise away from the sandy shores of Aqaba, the water transitions from turquoise to deep sapphire. Looking down through the glass, you will spot colorful coral gardens swarming with exotic fish, including clownfish, parrotfish, and graceful rays. The captain navigates over famous sites, including a sunken tank and a shipwreck, explaining the marine life as you go.
data.As you reach the end of the Siq, th: As you reach the end of the Siq, the narrow slit in the rocks suddenly opens to reveal The Treasury (Al-Khazneh), illuminated by over 1,500 candles spread across the sandy floor. The sight is nothing short of breathtaking. Visitors are invited to sit on rugs scattered across the ground, served traditional sweet Bedouin tea, and treated to a hauntingly beautiful performance of traditional music played on the Rababa (a stringed instrument) and the Ney (a wooden flute).
data.Ascenso a El Deir (El Monasterio) p: Ascent to El Deir (The Monastery) through the 850 steps carved in rock. Stunning desert views.

data.Beginning in Cairo, you will witnes: Beginning in Cairo, you will witness the majestic Giza Pyramids and the newly opened Grand Egyptian Museum. You will then trace Coptic routes through historic sites like Tell Basta, Wadi El Natrun, and the Coptic Quarter, before driving south to El Minya and Asiut to explore sacred mountaintop monasteries.
data.Beyond Karnak and the Valley of the: Beyond Karnak and the Valley of the Kings lie incredible, lesser-known temples waiting to be explored. Discover the secrets of ancient Thebes without the crowds.
data.Beyond the extraordinary accommodat: Beyond the extraordinary accommodation, the camp offers a full luxury experience. Guests dine in a communal luxury tent or outdoor terrace, enjoying traditional Zarb (meat and vegetables slow-cooked in a sand pit). In the morning, you wake up to the sun illuminating the massive sandstone cliffs right outside your window, enjoying a gourmet breakfast before heading out on your desert adventures.
data.Beyond the views, Mena House offers: Beyond the views, Mena House offers world-class amenities including a massive outdoor pool surrounded by landscaped gardens, a luxurious spa, and exceptional dining. The legendary 139 Pavilion restaurant serves exquisite international and local cuisine, allowing guests to dine outdoors while the Pyramids are illuminated against the night sky.

data.Café da manhã no Hotel, passeio de: Breakfast at the hotel, half-day tour to the Pyramids of Giza: Cheops, Chephren, and Mykerinos (does not include entry inside the pyramids). Next, visit the pyramids viewpoint, time for photos, then walk to see the Sphinx. Afterwards, visit an original Papyrus factory. Then, a guided tour of the Grand Egyptian Museum (GEM) offers an immersive experience, including the facade with the statue of Ramesses II, the Grand Staircase, access to the permanent galleries like Tutankhamun's collection, Cheops' solar boat, and the commercial area. Return to the hotel. Remainder of the day free. Overnight stay.

On this day, we will also visit a fragrance factory, the essences of many French perfumes. Dinner on board. Overnight stay.
Breakfast at the hotel and free day. Overnight stay.
data.Café da manhã no hotel e saída ao A: Breakfast at the hotel and transfer to the airport for your international return flight to your home country. End of our services.
data.Café da manhã no hotel e saída cedo: Breakfast at the hotel and early departure from Cairo by land towards Alexandria. Situated on the shores of the Mediterranean Sea, it was the capital of ancient Egypt for over 1000 years and famous for its ancient lighthouse (one of the 7 wonders of the ancient world), in whose place the Citadel of Qaitbay stands today. We will also visit the Catacombs of Kom el Shoqafa, Pompey's Pillar (a monument carved from a single piece of red granite weighing 396 tons), and the Montazah Palace. Additionally, we will visit the famous Library of Alexandria, one of the largest and most important in the world (closed on weekends – FRI / SAT). Stop for included lunch at a local restaurant (excluding drinks). Return to the hotel in Cairo. Dinner is on your own (not included) and overnight stay.
data.Café da manhã no hotel e saída para: Breakfast at the hotel, followed by a visit to the New Museum (Grand Egyptian Museum), the Alabaster Mosque, and the Citadel of Saladin, a walking tour of the Coptic Quarter, and a visit to the Khan El-Khalili bazaar. Stop for lunch at a local restaurant. After lunch, return to the hotel; free time and dinner on your own. Overnight stay.

Breakfast at the hotel and departure to visit the New Museum, Alabaster Mosque and Saladin Citadel, followed by a walking tour through the Coptic Quarter and Khan El Khalili market. Stop for lunch at a local restaurant. After lunch, return to the hotel; dinner on your own. Overnight.

Breakfast at the hotel and departure to visit the Pyramids of Giza: Cheops, Chephren, and Mykerinos. Next, we will go to the viewpoint for the best photos of the Pyramids, and then visit the Sphinx (head of a Pharaoh, body of a Lion), guardian of the Pyramids. Afterwards, we will visit a Papyrus factory and head out to visit the New Museum. Return to the hotel and free afternoon.

Breakfast at the hotel, followed by a visit to the Giza Pyramids: Cheops, Chephren, and Mykerinos. Next, we will head to the panoramic viewpoint for the best photo opportunities, and then visit the Sphinx (pharaoh's head and lion's body), guardian of the Pyramids. Afterward, we will visit a papyrus factory and then proceed to visit the New Museum. Return to the hotel and free afternoon.

Breakfast at the hotel and departure to visit the Giza Pyramids: Cheops, Chephren and Mykerinos. Next we head to the panoramic viewpoint for the best photo opportunity, then visit the Sphinx — pharaoh's head and lion's body, guardian of the Pyramids. Next stop: Memphis and Saqqara. Memphis, the first capital of Egypt, where we see the oldest pyramid, the Step Pyramid of Djoser. Stop for lunch at a local restaurant (drinks not included). Then visit the mastabas (nobles' tombs) and the Pyramid of Teti. At the end of the tour, we stop at a papyrus factory. Return to the hotel; dinner on your own. Overnight.

Breakfast at the hotel, followed by a visit to the Giza Pyramids: Cheops, Chephren, and Mykerinos. Next, we will head to the panoramic viewpoint for the best views of the Pyramids for photos, and then visit the Sphinx (pharaoh's head and lion's body), guardian of the Pyramids. Next stop: Memphis and Sakkara. Memphis was the country's first capital, and in Sakkara, we will see the oldest pyramid in Egypt, the Step Pyramid of Djoser. Stop for lunch at a local restaurant (excluding drinks). Afterward, we will visit the mastabas (tombs of the nobles) and the Pyramid of Teti. At the end of the tour, we will stop at a papyrus factory. Return to the hotel; dinner is on your own (not included). Overnight stay.

Breakfast at the hotel and departure to visit the Giza pyramids: Cheops, Chephren, and Mykerinos. data.Desayuno en el hotel. Por la mañana: Breakfast at the hotel. In the morning, departure to see the ancient Walls of Constantinople, visit to Rüstem Pasha Mosque, designed by the imperial architect Mimar Sinan for the Grand Vizier Damat Rüstem Pasha. Visit to the Egyptian Bazaar or Spice Bazaar, built in the 17th century. Possibility of optional excursion 'Pearls of Istanbul': boat tour on the Bosphorus, visit to Taksim area, Taksim Square, Istiklal Street, Maksem Cistern, Armenian Catholic Church, Greek Orthodox Church, Flower Passage, Fish Market, Whirling Dervish school and Galata Tower. Return to the hotel at the client's expense. Meals: Breakfast.

Arrival in the capital and visit to the Mausoleum of Ataturk, dedicated to the founder of the Turkish Republic. Dinner and accommodation at the hotel. Meals: Breakfast and dinner.
data.Desayuno en el hotel. Salida para C: Breakfast at the hotel. Departure to Cappadocia passing by the salt lake. Visit to the underground cities built by Christian communities to protect themselves from Arab attacks, with stables, common rooms, meeting halls and small rooms. Arrival at the hotel. Visit to a ceramics and carpet workshop. Free afternoon. Dinner and accommodation at the hotel. Meals: Breakfast and dinner.

Visit to the House of the Virgin Mary, a place of pilgrimage. Stop at a leather production center with a fashion show. Dinner and accommodation. Meals: Breakfast and dinner.

data.Descubra la grandeza de Turquía en: Discover the greatness of Turkey on an 11-day tour combining history, culture and unique landscapes. data.Descubra o Egito em um roteiro comp: Discover Egypt on a complete eleven-day itinerary: explore the Pyramids of Giza, the New Museum and the temples of the Nile between Luxor and Aswan, and finish with three all-inclusive nights in Sharm El Sheikh. data.Descubra o Egito em um roteiro de n: Discover Egypt in a nine-day itinerary: explore the Pyramids of Giza, the New Museum, Alexandria on the Mediterranean and an unforgettable Nile cruise from Aswan to Luxor. A perfect combination of history, culture and fascinating landscapes.
data.Descubra o coração do Egito Antigo: Discover the heart of Ancient Egypt on this intense four-day itinerary. data.Finally, the Temple of Seti I at Gu: Finally, the Temple of Seti I at Gurna offers an intimate look at exquisite bas-reliefs and refined artistic techniques. data.Further off the traditional tourist: Further off the traditional tourist trail lies the Ramesseum, the memorial temple of Ramses II. While partially ruined, the sheer scale of the fallen colossal statue of the pharaoh—which inspired Percy Bysshe Shelley's famous poem 'Ozymandias'—is awe-inspiring. The romantic melancholy of these ruins, set against the backdrop of the Theban hills, makes it an essential stop for history enthusiasts and photographers alike.

data.L'Egitto nella sua essenza più pura: L'Egitto nella sua essenza più pura. Dalle Piramidi di Giza alle colonne di Karnak, passando per la crociera sul Nilo tra Aswan e Luxor, questo itinerario di otto giorni è il modo più elegante per scoprire la civiltà dei faraoni — con guida in italiano e trasferimenti inclusi.
data.L'equilibrio ideale tra l'antico Eg: The ideal balance between ancient Egypt and the beaches of the Red Sea in an exciting eleven-day journey. Start from the wonder of the Pyramids and a romantic cruise between Luxor and Aswan, concluding the trip with three all-inclusive nights in Sharm El Sheikh. Dive into transparent waters admiring the famous coral reef to crown your perfect holiday.

data.La jornada más épica de Oriente Med: The most epic journey of the Middle East in a single tour: four countries, four cultures, four civilizations. From the Ottoman mosques of Istanbul to the ultra-modern glitz of Dubai, the pink magic of Petra and the timeless temples of the Nile, this 19-day tour is the definitive compendium of the best the East has to offer.
data.La versione più flessibile del nost: The most flexible version of our Nile–Red Sea itinerary. data.Life on board is pure indulgence. S: Life on board is pure indulgence. Spend your afternoons lounging by the rooftop plunge pool, watching the timeless scenes of farmers and fishermen on the lush riverbanks. Evenings feature gourmet dining in elegant restaurants, followed by entertainment ranging from traditional Nubian music to Galabeya dress-up parties. It is a seamless, highly romantic way to travel through history.

data.Luxor, often described as the world: Luxor, often described as the world's greatest open-air museum, is undeniably famous for the colossal Karnak Temple and the pharaohs' tombs in the Valley of the Kings. However, for the discerning traveler, the true magic of this ancient city lies off the beaten path, hidden amongst the sugar cane fields and lesser-visited desert valleys. These secondary temples offer a profound, uncrowded connection to Egypt's majestic past.
data.Luxury glamping has transformed the: Luxury glamping has transformed the Wadi Rum experience. You no longer need to sacrifice comfort to immerse yourself in the wilderness. High-end camps offer geodesic dome tents—often referred to as 'Martian Domes'—featuring transparent panoramic panels. From the comfort of a king-sized bed adorned with premium linens, you can watch the sun set the desert ablaze in reds and purples before giving way to the darkest, most star-filled sky you will ever witness.

data.Mornings in Wadi Rum are equally ma: Mornings in Wadi Rum are equally majestic. Waking up early to take a silent camel ride into the desert as the sun rises, casting long, dramatic shadows across the canyons, is an unforgettable experience. Whether you spend your days hiking through narrow gorges, scrambling up rock bridges, or simply meditating in the vast emptiness, camping in Wadi Rum connects you to nature in its most raw and beautiful form.
data.Most travelers opt for a short suns: Most travelers opt for a short sunset sail in Aswan or Luxor, which is a magical experience as the sky turns brilliant shades of gold and crimson. However, for the truly adventurous, multi-day felucca journeys are available. These trips involve sleeping on the deck under a blanket of stars and eating simple, delicious meals prepared by your Nubian crew over a small stove. It is a rustic experience, but one that offers unparalleled serenity.
data.Mount Nemrut in southeastern Turkey: Mount Nemrut in southeastern Turkey is home to one of the most mysterious and awe-inspiring archaeological sites in the world. At the 2,134-meter summit lies the tomb-sanctuary of King Antiochus I, flanked by massive, decapitated stone heads of ancient gods. Camping near the summit provides the unique opportunity to witness the legendary Mount Nemrut sunrise without having to make the grueling middle-of-the-night drive from the city.

data.Navegue pela grandiosidade do Egito: Sail through the grandeur of ancient Egypt over eight unforgettable days with a five-star Nile River cruise from Aswan to Luxor. Gaze at the magnitude of majestic temples like Philae, Karnak and Kom Ombo, and the mysterious beauty of the Valley of the Kings. The itinerary is complemented by the Pyramids of Giza and the New Egyptian Museum in Cairo, offering the authentic pharaonic essence.

Navigate the grandeur of ancient Egypt during eight unforgettable days, with a Nile River cruise from Aswan to Luxor. Contemplate majestic temples such as Philae, Karnak, and Kom Ombo, and the Valley of the Kings. The itinerary is complemented by the Pyramids of Giza and the Grand Egyptian Museum in Cairo.
data.Navigate the labyrinthine alleys of: Navigate the labyrinthine alleys of one of the world's oldest and largest covered markets. From Turkish delight to antique kilims, here is your ultimate shopping guide.
data.Navigate through Sword and Rose val: Navigate through Sword and Rose valleys
data.Navigating the bazaar can feel over: Navigating the bazaar can feel overwhelming to the uninitiated. The key is to embrace the art of getting lost. The market is roughly organized by trade—one alley shimmers with gold jewelry, another is draped in vibrant textiles and carpets, while others are filled with the rich aromas of leather goods or the clinking of hand-painted ceramics. Allow your senses to guide you, and do not be afraid to wander off the main thoroughfares into the quieter, more specialized courtyards (han).

data.Nestled in the shadow of the Great: Nestled in the shadow of the Great Pyramids of Giza, the Marriott Mena House is not just a hotel; it is a vital part of Egyptian history. Originally built as a hunting lodge for Khedive Ismail in 1869, this palatial estate has hosted kings, emperors, and heads of state. The lush, sprawling gardens offer an oasis of tranquility just steps away from the ancient wonders of the world.
data.Night hike up Mount Sinai to witnes: Night hike up Mount Sinai to witness sunrise from the summit. After descending, coffee and cookies will be served, followed by a visit to St. Catherine's Monastery. Return to the hotel around 02:30 the following day.

data.No culinary tour of Turkey is compl: No culinary tour of Turkey is complete without indulging its sweet tooth. While Baklava—layers of paper-thin phyllo dough filled with crushed pistachios and drenched in syrup—is world-famous, you must also try Künefe. This incredible dessert consists of a mild, stretchy cheese encased in shredded kadayıf dough, baked until crispy, soaked in sweet syrup, and served hot so the cheese pulls in glorious strings. It is the perfect conclusion to a Turkish feast.

data.One such marvel is the Temple of Me: One such marvel is the Temple of Medinet Habu. Second only to Karnak in size, this magnificent mortuary temple of Ramses III is remarkably well-preserved. Its deeply carved hieroglyphs retain much of their original vibrant coloring, providing a vivid glimpse into ancient artistry. The towering pylons and massive courtyards are often devoid of the large tour groups, allowing visitors to wander in serene contemplation.

data.Shopping here is as much about the: Shopping here is as much about the social interaction as it is about the transaction. Haggling is expected and should be approached with a smile and a sense of humor. When a shopkeeper invites you in for a glass of apple tea (elma çayı), accept it! This hospitality is a cornerstone of Turkish commerce. Sit, sip, chat about your travels, and then begin the friendly negotiation. It is a dance that locals have perfected over centuries.

data.Sitting at 430 meters below sea lev: Sitting at 430 meters below sea level, the Dead Sea is the lowest point on the surface of the Earth. Bordering Jordan to the east, this landlocked body of water is one of the world's most unique natural phenomena and has been revered as a sanctuary of health and healing since the days of King Herod and Cleopatra. Today, it remains the ultimate destination for wellness-focused luxury travelers.
data.Situated on the edge of the famous: Situated on the edge of the famous salt lake at the lowest point on Earth, the Kempinski Hotel Ishtar Dead Sea offers an extraordinary wellness and luxury experience. The hotel's architecture is a stunning tribute to the Hanging Gardens of Babylon, featuring cascading terraces, grand stone facades, and olive trees that dot the expansive property.
