data.The 'Low Season' spans the blazing: The 'Low Season' spans the blazing summer months from May to September. Temperatures in Upper Egypt (Luxor and Aswan) can regularly exceed 40°C (104°F), making midday sightseeing incredibly difficult. However, if you are willing to start your tours at dawn and spend your afternoons relaxing in a 5-star hotel pool or escaping to the luxurious Red Sea resorts of Sharm El-Sheikh and Hurghada, you will be rewarded with virtually empty temples and significant discounts on premium accommodations.
data.The 'Peak Season' occurs during the: The 'Peak Season' occurs during the winter months of December, January, and February. During this time, the weather is incredibly pleasant, with daytime temperatures in Cairo and Luxor hovering around a comfortable 20°C to 25°C (68°F to 77°F). This is the absolute best weather for exploring the Valley of the Kings or wandering around the Giza plateau without suffering from heat exhaustion. However, because the weather is ideal, this is also when the crowds are thickest and luxury hotel rates are at their premium.
data.The 'Shoulder Seasons' of October/N: The 'Shoulder Seasons' of October/November and March/April are often considered the sweet spots. The weather is warm but not yet oppressive, and the massive winter crowds have either not yet arrived or have begun to disperse. This is an excellent time for a Nile Cruise, as you can comfortably lounge on the sun deck during the day and enjoy cool, breezy evenings. Note that late March and April can sometimes bring the 'Khamsin', a dry, dusty desert wind, but it is usually brief.
data.The Bosphorus Strait is the geograp: The Bosphorus Strait is the geographical and spiritual heart of Istanbul, slicing the city in half and separating the continents of Europe and Asia. A private or VIP sunset cruise on this legendary waterway is an unforgettable way to witness the sheer scale and beauty of the city. As the yacht departs from the pier, you are immediately flanked by centuries of history on both shores.
data.The Cave Church (Monastery of Saint: The Cave Church (Monastery of Saint Simon) in the Mokattam Mountain
data.The Grand Bazaar (Kapalıçarşı) in I: The Grand Bazaar (Kapalıçarşı) in Istanbul is not merely a market; it is a chaotic, colorful, and sensory labyrinth that has pulsed as the commercial heart of the city for over half a millennium. Spanning 61 covered streets and housing over 4,000 shops, it is one of the largest and oldest covered markets in the world. Stepping through its grand archways is like stepping into an Ottoman era time capsule.
data.The Red Sea coast of Aqaba boasts s: The Red Sea coast of Aqaba boasts some of the most pristine, vibrant coral reefs in the world. For those who want to experience this underwater marvel without scuba diving, a luxury Glass-Bottom Boat cruise is the perfect solution. These specialized vessels feature large, clear viewing panels set into the hull, offering a literal window into the bustling marine ecosystem below.
data.The Siq, a narrow 1.2-kilometer gor: The Siq, a narrow 1.2-kilometer gorge flanked by towering 80-meter-high sandstone cliffs, is lit only by the soft, flickering glow of paper lanterns lining the path. Walking through this winding canyon in near silence, accompanied only by the whisper of the wind and the crunch of gravel beneath your feet, builds an incredible sense of anticipation. It is a meditative walk that prepares you for the grand reveal.
data.The White Desert (Sahara el Beyda): The White Desert (Sahara el Beyda) is one of Egypt's best-kept secrets and offers a camping experience unlike anywhere else on Earth. Located far in the western desert near the Farafra Oasis, this protected national park is famous for its bizarre, chalk-white rock formations that have been sculpted by wind and sand over millions of years into shapes resembling mushrooms, chickens, and alien spires.
data.The climax of the safari occurs as: The climax of the safari occurs as the sun sets. The group arrives at a secluded Bedouin camp nestled in a valley. Here, you will be treated to authentic Bedouin hospitality, sipping sweet sage tea by the fire while a traditional feast is prepared. As darkness falls, the absolute lack of light pollution reveals a staggering canopy of stars, turning the desert into a spectacular natural observatory.
data.The crown jewel of the Kempinski Is: The crown jewel of the Kempinski Ishtar is its colossal spa, the largest in the Middle East. It features specialized hydro-facilities, Tepidarium heated lounges, and therapeutic massages utilizing local Dead Sea sea salt and mud. Dining is equally lavish, with multiple restaurants offering everything from authentic Levantine mezzes to fine Italian cuisine, all accompanied by spectacular sunset views.
data.The cruise is not just about viewin: The cruise is not just about viewing; it is about relaxing. The boats feature shaded upper decks where you can sunbathe and enjoy refreshing drinks while taking in views of the coastline, where the borders of Jordan, Israel, Egypt, and Saudi Arabia converge. Most cruises drop anchor at a calm reef, allowing guests to jump into the warm, clear water for a guided snorkeling session.
data.The day inevitably begins with the: The day inevitably begins with the legendary Turkish Breakfast (Kahvaltı). This is not merely a meal, but a lavish morning ritual. A traditional kahvaltı spread covers the entire table with small plates: a variety of cheeses (like salty feta and mild kashkaval), black and green olives, fresh tomatoes, cucumbers, thick clotted cream (kaymak) drenched in local honey, spicy sujuk (sausage), and endless glasses of black tea, all accompanied by freshly baked simit—a sesame-encrusted bread ring.
data.The evening experience in a Bedouin: The evening experience in a Bedouin camp is rich with culture. Traditional Zarb—a meal of meat and vegetables slow-cooked in an underground sand oven—is unearthed and served communally. Following dinner, guests gather around a crackling campfire, sipping sweet sage tea, listening to ancient Bedouin folklore, and staring up at the Milky Way. The silence of the desert at night is profound, offering a rare and deep sense of isolation and peace.
data.The evening is spent gathered aroun: The evening is spent gathered around a campfire. Your guides will prepare a delicious, hearty meal over the open flames, usually featuring grilled chicken, rice, and fresh vegetables. With zero light pollution for hundreds of miles, the stargazing is simply phenomenal. Falling asleep in the absolute silence of the desert, surrounded by the ghostly white rocks under the Milky Way, is an intensely moving experience.
data.The flight itself is incredibly pea: The flight itself is incredibly peaceful. Unlike a helicopter or airplane, a hot air balloon moves with the wind, resulting in a silent, motionless sensation of floating. As the sun begins to rise over the horizon, painting the sky in hues of peach, gold, and lavender, the full majesty of Cappadocia is revealed. Expert pilots skillfully navigate the balloons, sometimes rising thousands of feet for panoramic views, and other times dipping incredibly low, drifting just meters above the fruit orchards and weaving between the fairy chimneys.
data.The guided convoy travels through s: The guided convoy travels through some of Cappadocia's most iconic landscapes, including Sword Valley, Rose Valley, and Love Valley. You will weave directly between the towering 'fairy chimneys'—bizarre, mushroom-shaped rock formations created by volcanic eruptions and millennia of erosion. The route includes several stops at panoramic viewpoints perfect for photography.
data.The hotel's amenities are legendary: The hotel's amenities are legendary. An incredible heated infinity pool gives the illusion of floating directly in the Bosphorus. The Sanitas Spa offers an authentic, hyper-luxurious Turkish Hammam experience. For dining, the award-winning Tuğra Restaurant, located on the first floor of the historical palace, serves ultimate Ottoman dining experiences against a backdrop of breathtaking waterside views.
data.The image of hundreds of colorful h: The image of hundreds of colorful hot air balloons ascending simultaneously over the surreal, lunar-like landscape of Cappadocia is one of travel's most iconic visuals. This central Turkish region, famous for its 'fairy chimneys' (tall, cone-shaped rock formations), ancient cave dwellings, and underground cities, is arguably the best place in the world for hot air ballooning. For the luxury traveler, this is an absolute, non-negotiable bucket list experience.
data.The interior of the bubble tent is: The interior of the bubble tent is the epitome of glamping. You will find a plush king-sized bed positioned perfectly to face the transparent wall. As night falls, you can literally lie in bed and watch the stars traverse the incredibly dark desert sky. Curtains can be drawn for privacy, but leaving them open to the cosmos is the true highlight of the stay.
data.The luxury sunset cruise experience: The luxury sunset cruise experience is elevated by exceptional hospitality. Guests are served a gourmet dinner featuring Turkish meze and fresh seafood, accompanied by premium local wines. As darkness falls, the magnificent Bosphorus Bridges light up, connecting the continents with ribbons of neon. The evening often concludes with refined traditional entertainment, making it a highly romantic and culturally rich night out.
data.The magic begins before dawn. You a: The magic begins before dawn. You are picked up from your luxury cave hotel while the stars are still out and driven to the launch site. As you sip hot coffee in the crisp morning air, you watch the massive nylon envelopes inflate, glowing like giant lightbulbs against the dark landscape as the burners roar to life. The anticipation builds until you step into the woven wicker basket and gently, almost imperceptibly, leave the earth behind.
data.The most famous aspect of the Dead: The most famous aspect of the Dead Sea is, of course, the buoyancy. The water is nearly ten times saltier than the ocean. This extreme hypersalinity means that swimming is practically impossible; instead, you effortlessly bob on the surface like a cork. Reading a newspaper or a book while floating on your back is the quintessential Dead Sea photo opportunity. However, it is the composition of the water, rich in magnesium, calcium, potassium, and bromide, that provides remarkable therapeutic benefits for the skin and joints.
data.The most popular time for this safa: The most popular time for this safari is late afternoon. As the tour progresses, the setting sun casts long shadows and illuminates the tuff rock in brilliant shades of pink and rose. The tour finishes at a spectacular sunset observation point, allowing riders to turn off their engines, soak in the profound silence of the valleys, and watch the colors shift across the Anatolian plains.
data.The pace of life on the Nile is sed: The pace of life on the Nile is sedate and majestic. During the day, you will disembark to visit monumental sites alongside an expert Egyptologist guide. You will explore the vast Karnak Temple, the beautifully preserved Temple of Horus at Edfu, and the unique double temple of Kom Ombo. Returning to the ship, you are greeted with cool towels and refreshing drinks.
data.The resort blends classical charm w: The resort blends classical charm with modern luxury. Guest rooms feature rich fabrics, elegant traditional furnishings, and private balconies that frame the majestic Pyramids perfectly against the skyline. Whether you are enjoying a morning espresso or a sunset cocktail, the proximity to the ancient monuments is an unparalleled, surreal experience.
data.The resort boasts an impressive arr: The resort boasts an impressive array of freshwater pools, including stunning infinity edge pools that seem to melt directly into the Dead Sea horizon. Guests have exclusive access to a private, pristine stretch of the Dead Sea shore, complete with attendants providing bowls of the famous mineral-rich black mud for natural therapeutic treatments under the Jordanian sun.
data.The ritual of the Dead Sea experien: The ritual of the Dead Sea experience involves more than just the water. The shores are lined with dark, mineral-rich mud. Visitors are encouraged to scoop up this thick, velvety mud and slather it entirely over their bodies. After letting it bake and dry in the Jordanian sun—which is safer here due to the extra atmospheric layer filtering out harmful UV rays—you wade back into the sea to wash it off. The result is incredibly soft, exfoliated, and glowing skin.
data.The route takes you past iconic lan: The route takes you past iconic landmarks that look best from the water. You will glide by the opulent Dolmabahçe and Çırağan palaces, the imposing Rumeli Fortress, and elegant waterfront mansions (Yalıs). As the sun begins to set, the sky turns brilliant shades of gold and crimson, silhouetting the minarets and domes of the Old City against the twilight.
data.The safari covers incredible geolog: The safari covers incredible geological formations, including massive natural rock bridges like the Um Frouth Rock Bridge, which adventurous guests can scramble up for panoramic photos. You will also visit Khazali Canyon, a narrow fissure cutting into the mountainside, the walls of which are covered in ancient Nabatean, Islamic, and Thamudic petroglyphs.
data.The second half of your journey tak: The second half of your journey takes you to Jordan, starting from Madaba's famous mosaic map and Mount Nebo to the legendary city of Petra and the Martian landscapes of Wadi Rum, finishing with premium services and absolute comfort throughout.
data.The tour begins in Cairo with an ex: The tour begins in Cairo with an exclusive, crowd-free morning visit to the Giza Plateau and the Grand Egyptian Museum, followed by a luxury domestic flight to Luxor to walk among the monumental pillars of Karnak and the Valley of the Kings.
data.The true reward comes hours before: The true reward comes hours before dawn. You will wake up in the pitch dark, bundle up, and make the final short hike to the summit terraces. As the sun breaches the horizon, it slowly illuminates the massive stone faces of Apollo, Zeus, and Hercules, casting long, dramatic shadows. Watching the sunrise over the Mesopotamian plains alongside these silent, ancient sentinels is a deeply spiritual and unforgettable moment.
data.The ultimate luxury expedition cove: The ultimate luxury expedition covering Istanbul, Cappadocia, Konya, Pamukkale, and Ephesus.
data.The Çırağan Palace Kempinski is the: The Çırağan Palace Kempinski is the epitome of Istanbul luxury, occupying a genuine 19th-century Ottoman imperial palace right on the shores of the Bosphorus. It is the only Ottoman Imperial Palace and Hotel situated on the strait that divides Europe and Asia, offering guests an experience steeped in absolute royal grandeur.
data.There are few travel experiences as: There are few travel experiences as universally acclaimed yet deeply moving as Petra by Night. Operating only three times a week—Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays—this after-dark excursion transforms the already mystical ancient Nabatean city into a scene straight out of a desert fairy tale. The journey begins at the visitor center, but the true magic starts as you step into the Siq.
data.This is an adventure camping experi: This is an adventure camping experience. Campsites are established at a safe, designated altitude just below the summit's archaeological zone. The air here is crisp and cold, even in summer, so high-quality, insulated tents and thick sleeping bags are provided. The evening involves a warm meal, hot Turkish tea, and sleeping under a vast, unpolluted sky.
data.This package includes seamless tran: This package includes seamless transition transfers from your cultural tour directly to the resort, creating a beautifully balanced holiday experience.
data.Thrilling off-road adventures with: Thrilling off-road adventures with premium SUVs and expert drivers.

data.Timing is everything when planning: Timing is everything when planning a journey to Egypt. With a climate defined by scorching desert summers and mild, pleasant winters, choosing the right month can drastically alter your experience at open-air historical sites. The general consensus is that the prime tourist season runs from October to April, but each season offers its own unique advantages and challenges for the luxury traveler.
data.To ensure a premium experience, it: To ensure a premium experience, it is crucial to book a 'Comfort' or 'VIP' flight. Standard baskets can hold up to 28 people, which can feel crowded. Luxury options limit the basket to 8-12 passengers, providing ample space to move around and take unobstructed photographs. Flights are highly dependent on weather and wind conditions, so it is highly recommended to book your flight for your very first morning in Cappadocia, allowing backup days in case of cancellation.
data.To make the most of this experience: To make the most of this experience, arrive early to secure a good spot, and remember to respect the silence of the canyon. Photography can be challenging in the extreme low light; bring a tripod if you wish to capture the scene, but also remember to put the camera down and simply absorb the atmosphere. Petra by Night is not just a visual spectacle; it is a spiritual journey back in time.
data.To maximize your experience, book a: To maximize your experience, book a stay at one of the luxury resorts that line the Jordanian coast. These properties offer private beach access, freshwater pools (essential after the intense sting of the salt water), and world-class spas that utilize the local minerals in their high-end treatments. The Dead Sea is not just a geological oddity; it is a restorative retreat for the mind, body, and soul.

A trip that brings together history, desert and luxury in perfect proportions.

data.Turkish cuisine is a magnificent ta: Turkish cuisine is a magnificent tapestry woven from the historical influences of the Ottoman Empire, bridging Central Asian traditions with Mediterranean and Middle Eastern flavors. It is a culinary culture that reveres fresh ingredients, masterful spicing, and the joy of communal dining. A journey to Turkey is as much an exploration of taste as it is of history, and missing out on its authentic dishes is missing out on the country's soul.

data.Uma imersão rápida e intensa no cor: A quick and intense immersion in the heart of Ancient Egypt. In four days you can contemplate the majestic Pyramids of Giza, the ancient Sphinx, the mysteries of Memphis and Sakkara and the fascinating Khan El Khalili Market — all with a guide in Portuguese and transfers included.
data.Uma jornada de cinco dias que une o: A five-day journey that unites the pharaonic treasures of Cairo with the Mediterranean breeze of Alexandria. Visit the Giza Pyramid complex and the legendary Sakkara Necropolis before heading to the coast. In Alexandria, admire the Catacombs of Kom El Shoqafa and the imposing Qaitbay Fortress, enjoying the rich gastronomy and cultural heritage on a fascinating itinerary.
data.Uma odisséia espetacular de nove di: A spectacular nine-day odyssey that explores the waters of the Mediterranean and the mysteries of the Nile Valley. From the Greco-Roman charm of the Library of Alexandria to the luxurious cruise that cruises from Aswan to Luxor, you will discover striking contrasts. Visits to the famous Pyramids and immersion in Coptic heritage transform this trip into a true living compendium of Egyptian history.
data.Un itinerario flessibile e affascin: A flexible and fascinating eleven-day itinerary, perfect for those seeking history and freedom on the Red Sea. Experience the essence of ancient Egypt by sailing the Nile and admiring the majestic Giza Plateau. Continue your stay in comfort at Hurghada's fabulous resorts, enjoying the freedom to modulate and choose excursions and optional activities to your liking.
data.Un viaggio esclusivo curato nei min: Un viaggio esclusivo curato nei minimi dettagli per il massimo comfort.
data.Un viaje espiritual e histórico inc: An incomparable 10-day spiritual and historical journey, ideal for walking in the footsteps of eternal civilizations. See the enigmatic Sphinx of Giza and sail along the Nile River admiring Luxor and Karnak. The journey culminates in Jerusalem and Bethlehem, exploring holy sites such as the Via Dolorosa and the Holy Sepulchre, and floating in the unique waters of the Dead Sea.
data.Un viaje profundamente significativ: A deeply meaningful journey that combines the pharaonic splendor of Egypt with the spirituality of the Holy Land. From the Pyramids and the Nile Cruise to Jerusalem, Bethlehem and the Dead Sea: ten days to tour the most sacred places of three great monotheistic religions with a guide in Spanish.
data.Un viaje que fusiona dos de las civ: A trip that merges two of humanity's most influential civilizations and offers an unforgettable experience. From the majestic Pyramids and the legendary Nile Cruise to the Acropolis of Athens and the Greek Islands Cruise: eleven days to explore 7,000 years of history in total comfort and a guide in Spanish.

A journey that merges two of humanity's most influential civilizations. From the majestic Pyramids and the legendary Nile Cruise to the Acropolis of Athens and the Greek Islands Cruise: eleven days to explore 7,000 years of history in total comfort and a guide in Spanish.
data.Un viaje que fusiona el alma árabe: A trip that merges the Arab soul of two continents: the mystique of Morocco—with its labyrinthine medinas, the majestic Hassan II Mosque and Jemaa el Fna Square—and the eternity of Egypt with the Nile Cruise and the Pyramids. Special departure from the Costa del Sol crossing by ferry to Tangier.
data.Una odisea espectacular de 19 días: A spectacular 19-day odyssey that intertwines the magic of Türkiye, the mystery of Jordan, the majesty of Egypt and the dazzling futurism of Dubai. Explore the ancient formations of Petra and sail four nights along the Nile before experiencing Bedouin safaris in the deserts of the emirate, always accompanied by guides in Spanish for an exceptional experience.
data.Una travesía grandiosa de 16 días d: A grandiose 16-day journey discovering the immortal wonders of Jordan, Egypt and Dubai. Delve into the Siq of Petra, cruise the mythical Nile River for four days and be impressed by the dazzling Sheikh Zayed Mosque in Abu Dhabi. A monumental tour designed to provide maximum comfort and authentic cultural immersion.

data.Vivencie o esplendor da civilização: Experience the splendour of Egyptian civilisation over nine unforgettable days, cruising the Nile from Luxor to Aswan. Marvel at majestic temples including Karnak, Philae, Horus at Edfu, Sobek at Kom Ombo, and the mystical Valley of the Kings. The itinerary also embraces the charm of Cairo with the Pyramids of Giza, the Grand Egyptian Museum (GEM), the Alabaster Mosque, and the vibrant Khan El Khalili Market.

Experience the splendor of Egyptian civilization over nine intense days, sailing the River Nile on the stunning route from Luxor to Aswan. Admire colossal constructions such as the Temple of Horus in Edfu and the mystical Valley of the Kings. The itinerary also embraces the charm of Islamic Cairo, with visits to the Alabaster Mosque and the Egyptian Museum, ensuring a rich and complete cultural immersion.

data.Wadi Rum has redefined desert campi: Wadi Rum has redefined desert camping with the introduction of 'Martian Domes' or Bubble Tents. These high-tech, luxury geodesic domes are designed to blend into the red desert landscape while providing 5-star hotel comfort. Featuring massive transparent panoramic panels, these tents allow you to immerse yourself in the desert environment without sacrificing air conditioning or a private en-suite bathroom.
data.Wadi Rum, famously known as the Val: Wadi Rum, famously known as the Valley of the Moon and the filming location for Lawrence of Arabia and The Martian, is best explored via a private, open-air jeep safari. This journey takes you deep into the protected desert wilderness, far beyond the reach of standard tourist buses, allowing you to experience the sheer scale and silence of this UNESCO World Heritage site.
data.Wadi Rum, known as the Valley of th: Wadi Rum, known as the Valley of the Moon, is a landscape of such alien beauty that it has served as the backdrop for countless science fiction films. Located in southern Jordan, this vast expanse of crimson sand and towering, monolith-like sandstone mountains offers one of the most spectacular desert environments on earth. However, a daytime jeep tour only scratches the surface; to truly understand Wadi Rum, you must spend the night.

data.When booking a felucca, especially: When booking a felucca, especially for overnight trips, ensure you go through a reputable luxury travel concierge to guarantee a well-maintained boat and a highly experienced captain. While you forfeit air conditioning and private bathrooms, the reward is an intimate, eco-friendly, and profoundly peaceful immersion into the very soul of Egypt.
data.When it comes to main courses, the: When it comes to main courses, the kebab reigns supreme, but the variety goes far beyond the familiar döner. The Adana Kebab, named after the southern city, is a masterpiece of spicy minced lamb grilled on a wide skewer over charcoal. For something richer, the İskender Kebab from Bursa features thin slices of grilled lamb basted with hot tomato sauce over pieces of pita bread, generously slathered with melted sheep's milk butter and served with yogurt.
data.When looking for authentic souvenir: When looking for authentic souvenirs, seek out high-quality Turkish towels (peshtemals), intricate Iznik ceramics, or a genuine hand-woven kilim rug. Be wary of cheap imports and always ask the vendor about the origin and craftsmanship of their wares. Whether you leave with an antique brass lantern or simply a bag of freshly dusted Turkish delight, the true treasure of the Grand Bazaar is the unforgettable experience of the marketplace itself.
data.Whether you need seamless airport t: Whether you need seamless airport transfers, inter-city travel, or a dedicated private chauffeur for your entire vacation, our luxury transportation program guarantees safety, punctuality, and style.
data.While hot air balloons offer a maje: While hot air balloons offer a majestic view from above, the Cappadocia ATV Safari provides an adrenaline-pumping, up-close exploration of this bizarre and beautiful landscape. Riding an All-Terrain Vehicle (quad bike) allows you to effortlessly navigate the dusty, undulating dirt trails that wind through the region's famous valleys, which are inaccessible to larger vehicles.
data.While multi-story luxury cruise shi: While multi-story luxury cruise ships offer undeniable comfort, there is no more authentic or peaceful way to experience the Nile River than aboard a traditional felucca. These simple, wooden sailing boats, distinguished by their lateen (triangular) sails, have navigated the waters of the Nile since the time of the pharaohs. A felucca trip strips away the noise and haste of modern travel, replacing it with the gentle lap of water and the whispering of the wind.
data.With seamless airport transfers fro: With seamless airport transfers from Cairo, this extension offers the perfect balance of relaxation and adventure along the stunning Sinai coast.
With seamless airport transfers from Cairo, this extension offers the perfect mix of relaxation and adventure on the stunning Red Sea coast.

data.athens_guided_tour_header: Specially created for lovers of deep archaeology, this premium ten-day itinerary takes you beyond the postcards. Experience all the grandeur of a Nile River cruise, explore mystical temples, and take part in a rare experience: an overnight stay in Abu Simbel with its indescribable night sound and light show, honoring the powerful Ramesses II.

data.classic_egypt_ii_title: Enjoy a smooth and comfortable journey with our private transfer services. Our professional drivers and representatives will be waiting for you at the airport, hotel, or any requested location to ensure a safe and hassle-free experience.
data.completoEgypt.day1.desc: On the first day, we will wait for you at the Cairo airport to deliver your visa, help you through immigration control, and take you to the hotel. In the evening, we will have dinner at a local restaurant. If you arrive after 21:00 hours, instead of dinner, we will offer a cold picnic at the hotel.
data.completoEgypt.day1.title: Arrival in Cairo
data.completoEgypt.day2.desc: After breakfast, we will visit the Giza pyramids and the sphinx, and get to know the interior of one of the pyramids. Upon finishing the visit, we will visit a papyrus institute to learn about the production of this excellent Egyptian product. To recharge batteries, we will have a traditional lunch and head to Memphis, the first capital of Egypt, to admire Saqqara, its necropolis, and the famous statue of Ramesses II. After stopping to rest at the hotel, in the evening we will go to ancient Cairo, the medieval area of the city, where we will stroll through its oldest street, walk through the Khan el Khalili market, and go to the famous Mirror Café and savor a typical dinner.
data.completoEgypt.day2.title: Pyramids, Sphinx & Memphis
data.completoEgypt.day3.desc: On this day we will visit the most emblematic places in downtown Cairo. We will walk through the Citadel of Saladin, get to know the Alabaster Mosque, bargain in the Khan el Khalili market, stroll through the Coptic Quarter, make a stop for lunch, and be impressed by the works of the Egyptian Museum (access to the mummy room is not included). In the evening, we will have dinner at a local restaurant.
data.completoEgypt.day3.title: Cairo City Tour
data.completoEgypt.day4.desc: After breakfast, we will go to the airport to catch the plane to Luxor, the ancient Thebes and the city from which the cruise departs. In this fascinating destination, we will visit the Luxor Temple - an unforgettable image - and tour the city center aboard a carriage driven by horses. Finally, we will take you to the cruise where we will rest until the next day.
data.completoEgypt.day4.title: Flight to Luxor & Nile Cruise
data.completoEgypt.day5.desc: Early in the morning, we will go to visit the Karnak Temple, separated from the Luxor Temple by the Avenue of Sphinxes. We will continue the tour crossing to the west bank of the Nile to visit the Valley of the Kings, where the tombs of the most famous pharaohs are located (visiting the tomb of Tutankhamun has an extra cost of €16). We will also visit the temple of Hatshepsut and the magnificent Colossi of Memnon. Upon returning to the boat, we will have lunch and begin sailing towards Edfu. At dusk, you will enjoy the crossing of the Esna lock.
data.completoEgypt.day5.title: Karnak & Valley of the Kings
data.completoEgypt.day6.desc: We will start the day by visiting the Edfu Temple. This temple, dedicated to the god Horus, is the best-preserved temple in Egypt. After your visit, we will return to the cruise to continue sailing towards Kom Ombo. When we arrive, we will visit its temple, dedicated to the gods Sobek and Hareoris and with a location that makes it one of the most beautiful in the country. At the end, we will continue sailing towards Aswan. Important: the visit to Edfu is made by a carriage pulled by horses. If you wish to use another means of transportation, you must notify the guide the day before and pay €15 per person.
data.completoEgypt.day6.title: Edfu & Kom Ombo
data.completoEgypt.day7.desc: The day begins with a bus excursion to the Temples of Abu Simbel, the most famous in Egypt, saved from the waters of the Nile thanks to international collaboration. On the way back to Aswan, we will see the impressive Aswan Dam, one of the largest engineering projects of the 20th century. We will also visit the Philae Temple, located on a beautiful island and known as the Jewel of the Nile, and return to the cruise for lunch. In the afternoon, we will take a felucca ride (the traditional Egyptian boat) and return to the boat at 16:00 hours. You will have free time for the rest of the afternoon and we will also sleep on the boat that night.
data.completoEgypt.day7.title: Abu Simbel & Aswan
data.completoEgypt.day8.desc: After breakfast, we will take you to the Aswan airport, from where you will take the return flight to Cairo. There at the Cairo airport, you can catch your international flight. We will adapt the departure time from Aswan according to the schedule of your international flight. If it leaves early from Cairo, we will fly from Aswan as early as possible and offer a continental or to-go breakfast. If your return flight is in the afternoon, you will not need to wake up so early and we will offer breakfast on the boat before taking you to Aswan to catch the flight to Cairo.
data.completoEgypt.day8.title: Departure from Cairo

data.completoEgypt.overview: An 8-day comprehensive journey through Egypt's greatest treasures. From the Pyramids of Giza to the temples of Abu Simbel, experience the full splendor of ancient Egypt with Nile cruise, expert guides, and all-inclusive comfort.

data.gre001.day1.desc: Arrival at the airport and meet with the agency assistance. Transfer to the hotel. Rest of the day free. Accommodation.
data.gre001.day1.title: Arrival / Athens
data.gre001.day2.desc: Breakfast and departure by coach to tour the most famous monuments of Athens. During the tour we pass by the Parliament with the monument of the Unknown Soldier, Syntagma Square, the neoclassical Trilogy, Omonia Square, the 19th-century Parliament building, the Catholic Cathedral, the Roman Temple of Zeus, Hadrian's Arch and stop for photos at the famous Olympic Stadium where the first modern Olympic Games were held in 1896. Visit of the Acropolis with the Propylaea, the Temple of Athena Nike, the Parthenon and the Erechtheion. Free time to explore the old quarter of Plaka. Accommodation.
data.gre001.day2.title: City Tour / Athens
data.gre001.day3.desc: Breakfast and transfer to the port of Piraeus to board the ferry bound for Mykonos. Upon arrival on the island, transfer to the hotel and rest of the day free. Accommodation.
data.gre001.day3.title: Piraeus – Mykonos / Mykonos
data.gre001.day4.desc: Free day on the famous island of Mykonos, the international meeting point of the "jet set". Enjoy the wonderful beaches, dazzling shops and incomparable nightlife. Breakfast and accommodation at the hotel.
data.gre001.day4.title: Free Day / Mykonos
data.gre001.day5.desc: Breakfast and free morning until the transfer time to the port of Mykonos to board the ferry bound for Santorini. Upon arrival on the island, transfer to the hotel and rest of the day free. Accommodation.
data.gre001.day5.title: Mykonos – Santorini / Santorini
data.gre001.day6.desc: Free day to explore the magical island of Santorini. Discover Fira & Oia with the stunning views of the volcano, and enjoy one of the best sunsets in the Aegean Sea. Explore the unique black sand beaches or take a boat tour to the caldera islands.
data.gre001.day6.title: Free Day / Santorini
data.gre001.day7.desc: Breakfast and transfer to the port of Santorini to board the high-speed ferry bound for Heraklion. Upon arrival on the island, transfer to the hotel and rest of the day free. Accommodation.
data.gre001.day7.title: Santorini – Heraklion / Crete
data.gre001.day8.desc: Free day to explore the largest of the Greek islands. Be sure to try the unique Cretan food, explore the Minoan civilization at Knossos, discover the beautiful beaches of Elounda, and enjoy a boat trip to the historic Spinalonga.
data.gre001.day8.title: Free Day / Crete
data.gre001.day9.desc: Breakfast and free morning until the transfer time to the airport for a short domestic flight, connecting with your international return flight.
data.gre001.day9.title: Departure

data.itinerary_item_2: Breakfast on board. Check-out at 08:00. Full day in Luxor: Karnak and Luxor Temples, Colossi of Memnon, Temple of Hatshepsut, Valley of the Kings.
data.itinerary_item_3: Optional: Saladin Citadel, Alabaster Mosque, Khan el Khalili, Egyptian Museum or Coptic Quarter.
data.itinerary_item_4: Breakfast. Guided visit to the Acropolis: the majestic Parthenon and the Erechtheion with its famous Caryatids.
data.itinerary_item_5: Panoramic tour of Athens: Tomb of the Unknown Soldier, Hellenic Parliament, Panathenaic Stadium (venue of the first modern Olympic Games).
data.itinerary_item_6: On board: orchestra, singers, traditional Greek dancers, and delicious Greek food included.
data.itinerary_item_7: Suggestions: Visit the Acropolis Museum, Plaka neighborhood, Ancient Agora or excursion to Cape Sounion.

data.jerusalem_old_city_via_dolorosa_and: Jerusalem: Old City, Via Dolorosa and Holy Sepulchre

data.karnak_luxor_east_bank: Breakfast on board. East bank: Karnak Temples (the largest religious complex in the ancient world) and Luxor Temple.
data.kom_ombo_sobek_haroeris: Breakfast on board. Navigation to Kom Ombo: visit to the temple of Sobek and Haroeris.
data.lunch_included_optional_abu_simbel_: Lunch included. Optional: Abu Simbel. Free afternoon in Aswan.
data.luxor_full_day_tour: Breakfast on board. Check-out at 08:00. Full day in Luxor: Karnak and Luxor Temples, Colossi of Memnon, Temple of Hatshepsut, Valley of the Kings.
data.luxor_west_bank_checkout: Breakfast. Check-out 08:00. Colossi of Memnon, Mortuary Temple of Hatshepsut, Valley of the Kings.
data.madaba_nebo_tour: Breakfast. St. George Orthodox Church in Madaba: the famous 6th-century mosaic map of Palestine. Mount Nebo: views of the Jordan Valley and the Dead Sea.
data.mct.estrellas.day10Desc: Morning flight from Amman to Cairo. Afternoon guided visit to the Giza Plateau: the Great Pyramids, the Sphinx, and the Valley Temple. Evening stroll at Khan El Khalili Bazaar. Overnight in Cairo.
data.mct.estrellas.day11Desc: Morning at the Grand Egyptian Museum (GEM). Afternoon visit to Coptic Cairo: the Hanging Church, the Ben Ezra Synagogue, and the Amr Ibn Al-As Mosque. Overnight in Cairo.
data.mct.estrellas.day12Desc: Morning flight from Cairo to Aswan. Afternoon visit to the Philae Temple on Agilkia Island and the High Dam. Enjoy a felucca ride at sunset around Elephantine Island and the Botanical Garden. Overnight in Aswan.
data.mct.estrellas.day13Desc: Early morning drive to Abu Simbel (approx. 3 hours south). Visit the magnificent temples of Ramesses II and Queen Nefertari. Return to Aswan for a free evening. Overnight in Aswan.
data.mct.estrellas.day14Desc: Check in to your Nile cruise ship. Sail north, stopping at Kom Ombo to visit the double temple dedicated to Sobek and Horus. Continue sailing to Edfu. Overnight on board.
data.mct.estrellas.day15Desc: Morning visit to the Temple of Horus at Edfu (by horse-drawn carriage). Arrive in Luxor and visit the Karnak Temple complex, the largest religious structure ever built. Evening light show at Karnak (optional). Overnight in Luxor.
data.mct.estrellas.day16Desc: Full-day exploration of the West Bank: Valley of the Kings, Temple of Hatshepsut at Deir el-Bahari, and the Colossi of Memnon. Afternoon at leisure. Overnight in Luxor.
data.mct.estrellas.day17Desc: Morning flight from Luxor to Dubai via Cairo. Upon arrival, a guided panoramic tour of the city: Burj Al-Arab, Palm Jumeirah, Atlantis The Palm, and Dubai Marina. Evening at leisure. Overnight in Dubai.
data.mct.estrellas.day18Desc: Morning free to enjoy the hotel or visit the Dubai Mall. Afternoon at the top of Burj Khalifa – the world’s tallest building. Evening 4×4 desert safari with dune bashing, camel ride, henna, and BBQ dinner under the stars.
data.mct.estrellas.day19Desc: After breakfast, free time for last shopping or a visit to the Gold Souk. Transfer to Dubai International Airport for your departure flight. End of services.
data.mct.estrellas.day1Desc: Welcome to İstanbul! Reception at the airport and private transfer to your hotel. The rest of the day is free to relax or begin exploring the city at your own pace. Overnight in İstanbul.
data.mct.estrellas.day2Desc: After breakfast, embark on a guided tour of the Sultanahmet district: the Hagia Sophia, Blue Mosque, Hippodrome, and Topkapi Palace. In the afternoon, dive into the labyrinthine Grand Bazaar. Evening Bosphorus dinner cruise with traditional music and dancing.
data.mct.estrellas.day3Desc: Morning Bosphorus cruise along the shores of Europe and Asia. Afternoon visit to the Spice Bazaar and the Dolmabahçe Palace. Free evening to explore the vibrant quarters of Beyoğlu and Taksim.
data.mct.estrellas.day4Desc: Early morning flight to Cappadocia. Upon arrival, visit the Kaymakli Underground City, the Uchisar Rock Castle, and the Pigeon Valley. Check in to a cave hotel and enjoy a traditional dinner.
data.mct.estrellas.day5Desc: Optional hot air balloon ride at sunrise. Then explore the Göreme Open-Air Museum, the Pasabag Fairy Chimneys, and the Devrent Valley. Enjoy a pottery workshop in Avanos. Overnight in Cappadocia.
data.mct.estrellas.day6Desc: Morning flight from Cappadocia to Amman. Afternoon guided visit to the magnificent Roman ruins of Jerash. Transfer to the hotel in Amman for dinner and overnight.
data.mct.estrellas.day7Desc: Full-day excursion south: visit Madaba to see the famous 6th-century mosaic map of the Holy Land, then Mount Nebo with its panoramic view of the Jordan Valley. Continue to the Dead Sea for a float experience. Return to Amman for overnight.
data.mct.estrellas.day7Title: Amman – Madaba, Mount Nebo & Dead Sea
data.mct.estrellas.day8Desc: Drive south to Petra. Enter through the Siq and marvel at the Treasury, the Royal Tombs, the Monastery (optional 800-step climb), and the Colonnaded Street. Dinner and overnight at a hotel near Petra.
data.mct.estrellas.day9Desc: Morning at leisure in Petra. Afternoon transfer to Wadi Rum. Enjoy a 4×4 jeep tour through the crimson desert and a traditional Bedouin dinner under the stars at a desert camp. Overnight in Wadi Rum.

data.mct.estrellas.highlight2: Bosphorus dinner cruise with traditional entertainment
data.mct.estrellas.highlight3: Guided tour of the ancient city of Petra – one of the New 7 Wonders of the World
data.mct.estrellas.highlight4: Cairo: Giza Pyramids, Sphinx, the Grand Egyptian Museum & Khan El Khalili Bazaar
data.mct.estrellas.highlight5: Nile felucca sailing at sunset in Aswan

data.mct.estrellas.overview: A grand 19-day odyssey spanning four iconic destinations: the mosaic crossroads of İstanbul, the rose-red city of Petra, the timeless pharaonic wonders of Cairo and the Nile, and the dazzling futuristic skyline of Dubai. This carefully curated itinerary blends ancient history, spiritual heritage, desert adventure, and ultra-modern luxury into one seamless, unforgettable journey.
data.mct.estrellas.subtitle: 19 Days – Türkiye, Jordan, Egypt & Dubai
data.mct.estrellas.title: Stars of the Middle East
data.mct001.day1.desc: Arrival, reception, and transfer to the hotel. Accommodation.
data.mct001.day1.title: PARENT COUNTRY - ISTANBUL
data.mct001.day10.desc: Full board. At the scheduled time, the visit to the eastern bank of Luxor will begin, with the Karnak Temple, which dominates the entire site of Thebes born from the splendor of the pharaohs of the New Kingdom, and the Luxor Temple, the work of two great pharaohs, Ramesses II and Ramesses III. Continuation of the visit to the western bank with the Necropolis of Thebes, including the Valley of the Kings where the tombs of the most important pharaohs of the New Kingdom are hidden, the Mortuary Temple of Queen Hatshepsut, and the giant and grandiose Colossi of Memnon. Return to the cruise ship, embarkation, and navigation towards Edfu. Night on board.
data.mct001.day10.title: NILE CRUISE (FULL BOARD)
data.mct001.day11.desc: Full board. At the scheduled time, visit to the Edfu Temple dedicated to the God Horus. Return to the cruise ship and navigation towards Kom Ombo. Arrival and visit to its Temple dedicated to the gods Sobek and Haroeris. Navigation towards Aswan. Night on board.
data.mct001.day11.title: NILE CRUISE (FULL BOARD)
data.mct001.day12.desc: Full board. Departure to visit the Philae Temple and a ride in a felucca (typical fishing boats) along the Nile, contemplating several volcanic islands of black granite. If you wish to take the optional visit to Abu Simbel, it is scheduled for this day. Night on board.
data.mct001.day12.title: NILE CRUISE (FULL BOARD)
data.mct001.day13.desc: After breakfast, disembarkation and check-out from the ship's cabins at 08:00 hrs, free time in Aswan. At the scheduled time, transfer to Aswan airport to take the flight to Cairo, transfer to the hotel, and the rest of the day free. Accommodation. There is an optional scheduled excursion to visit the most emblematic sites of the city: The Citadel of Saladin, Alabaster Mosque, Khan el-Khalili Market, Egyptian Museum, and Coptic Quarter accompanied by a guide and in private transport. Booking is possible at the destination.
data.mct001.day13.title: DISEMBARKATION - FLIGHT BOUND FOR CAIRO
data.mct001.day14.desc: Breakfast and check-out, and at the scheduled time, transfer to the airport to take the flight to Dubai. Arrival at Dubai International Airport. At this place, where thousands of passengers arrive each year, our representative will be waiting for you. Transfer to the chosen hotel and accommodation.
data.mct001.day14.title: CAIRO – DUBAI
data.mct001.day15.desc: Breakfast at the hotel. And half-day visit. Dubai is a city famous for its modernity, but it is important to know its history. This day we will have the opportunity to stroll through the old part of the city. Our first stop will be in the Bastakiya area; in this open-air museum, we can admire the small streets and their old Arabic-style houses with wind towers on each of them. We will enter one of the houses open to the public and learn a little about the customs and cultures of the local people. We will cross in an abra (handcrafted boat) to the spice and gold market. We will lose ourselves in the small streets enjoying the aroma and flavors to find the perfect souvenir to take home. In the shops of the gold market, you can find real jewelry in gold, platinum, and silver.
data.mct001.day15.title: DUBAI
data.mct001.day16.desc: Breakfast and visit to Abu Dhabi. This visit takes you to the capital Abu Dhabi, which is approximately 1 hour and 30 minutes from Dubai. On the way, we will pass by the Jebel Ali Port, which is the largest artificial port in the world. Upon arrival in the capital, our first stop will be at one of the largest mosques in the world. The Sheikh Zayed Mosque is a true architectural masterpiece, open to visitors from all over the world and of all religions. As we continue, we will take a panoramic tour passing by the palaces of the royal family of Abu Dhabi. Then a photo stop at the Emirates Palace and the Presidential Palace (from the outside). We continue along the beautiful Corniche (the promenade); we will make a stop at the cultural island of Abu Dhabi to photograph the Louvre Museum, opened in 2018. To conclude, we will tour Yas Island and make a stop to take photos at Ferrari World. Transfer to Dubai.
data.mct001.day16.title: DUBAI - ABU DHABI
data.mct001.day17.desc: Breakfast at the hotel and free morning. At 15:00, we will leave in modern 4x4 cars into the Dubai desert. Our English-speaking driver will take us on this adventure through the sand dunes; we will enjoy the most famous traditional ride of all. Women can wear brightly colored clothes and dresses to take photos at sunset; the best photos of couples and families can also be taken in this place. After sunset, our driver takes us to the camp where a delicious oriental buffet with a variety of dishes awaits us. During dinner, we will enjoy the oriental show, belly dancing, tanoura show, and also a fire show. At the end of the dinner, transfer back to the hotel.
data.mct001.day17.title: DUBAI
data.mct001.day18.desc: Breakfast at the hotel, free day in Dubai to make optional visits.
data.mct001.day18.title: ABU DHABI – DUBAI
data.mct001.day19.desc: Breakfast at the hotel. According to your flight schedule, we will leave the hotel 3 and a half hours before your flight departure time.
data.mct001.day19.title: DUBAI - DESTINATION
data.mct001.day2.desc: Breakfast. INCLUDED VISIT. Departure in the morning to visit the Prince's Mosque or the New Mosque (one of the two). Next, we will experience one of the most famous activities in Istanbul, a boat trip along the Bosphorus, the channel that separates Europe and Asia. During this journey, you can appreciate the palaces of the Sultans, old and typical wooden houses, and enjoy history in a different way. We will culminate our day with one of the star visits, the Spice Bazaar, established by the Ottomans 5 centuries ago and used ever since. We will enjoy free time. End of the visit at the Egyptian Bazaar.
data.mct001.day2.title: ISTANBUL
data.mct001.day3.desc: Breakfast. Free day. Free time to explore the city with the possibility of taking an optional full-day excursion "The Jewels of Constantinople" with lunch (€65): visit to the ancient Hippodrome belonging to the Byzantine era, which was the center of civil activity in the country and where not only horse races and gladiator fights took place, but also celebrations in honor of the Emperor. Today it preserves the Obelisk of Theodosius, the Serpentine Column, and the Fountain of Emperor Wilhelm. Blue Mosque, built in 1609 during the sultanate of Ahmet, who, wanting to surpass the Hagia Sophia Basilica with his mosque, built it with 6 minarets. Next, we will go to the Topkapi Palace, residence of the sultans until the 19th century, where most of the valuable objects of the Ottoman sultans are kept. The visit to Hagia Sophia, the most finished example of Byzantine art and one of humanity's greatest achievements. Next, transfer for the visit to the Grand Bazaar. Covered bazaar built in the year 1660 which can be considered as a small city covered by hundreds of domes, four thousand shops inside with alleys, squares, and 18 access gates. End of the visit at the Grand Bazaar.
data.mct001.day3.title: ISTANBUL B - L
data.mct001.day4.desc: Breakfast. Transfer to the airport (FLIGHT NOT INCLUDED) bound for Amman, arrival and transfer to the hotel, accommodation.
data.mct001.day4.title: ISTANBUL – AMMAN
data.mct001.day5.desc: Breakfast and departure to Madaba. Arrival and visit to the Church of Saint George, with its famous mosaic representing all the biblical territories. Continuation to Mount Nebo, from whose summit Moses glimpsed the Promised Land. Visit to the mosaic collection. Transfer for the visit to Shoubak Castle, sometimes referred to as “Mont Real”, Shoubak dates back to the same turbulent period as Karak. It is perched on the side of a mountain, over a wide area of fruit trees. The exterior of Shoubak Castle is impressive, with an imposing gate and a triple wall around it. Despite the precautions taken by its builder, the fortress fell into Saladin's hands only 75 years after its construction. The inscriptions of its proud successors appear on the castle walls. Continuation to Petra. Arrival in Petra. Dinner and accommodation at the hotel.
data.mct001.day5.title: AMMAN - MADABA - MOUNT NEBO - SHOUBAK CASTLE - PETRA
data.mct001.day6.desc: Breakfast. Day entirely dedicated to the visit of Petra, known as the “rose city”, where more than 2,000 years ago the Nabataeans located the capital of their empire for over 500 years, carving admirable temples and tombs into the pink mountains and using advanced agricultural and water conduction systems. The tour begins with the Tomb of the Obelisks, continuing through the Siq, a canyon more than 1 km long after which the Treasury is discovered, a colossal tomb decorated with columns and sculptures of incomparable refinement and beauty. Continuation towards the street of facades and the theater to approach the 850 steps that will lead us to the imposing Monasterio “El Deir”. Dinner and accommodation at the hotel.
data.mct001.day6.title: PETRA
data.mct001.day7.desc: ONWARDS) Breakfast. Visit Little Petra, which was inhabited by the Nabataeans and has many tombs, water containers, and channels; it has a small path leading to some of the inner area, Siq Al Bared. The scale of this area and the fact that it is the continuation of Petra gave it the name of Little Petra. Departure towards Wadi Rum. Excursion to the Wadi Rum desert and ride in 4x4 vehicles. We will enter the pink sands of this desert, which possesses a special charm provided by the granite massifs that nature has modeled into whimsical shapes. Return to Amman. Dinner and accommodation.
data.mct001.day7.title: PETRA - VISIT LITTLE PETRA - WADI RUM - AMMAN - CAIRO (FLIGHT NOT INCLUDED AND MUST BE AT NIGHT FROM 20:00 HRS
data.mct001.day8.desc: Breakfast. Transfer to the airport (FLIGHT NOT INCLUDED) bound for Cairo, arrival and transfer to the hotel, accommodation.
data.mct001.day8.title: AMMAN – CAIRO
data.mct001.day9.desc: Breakfast, in the morning scheduled visit to the site of the Pyramids of Cheops, Chephren, Mykerinus, and the Sphinx. This visit lasts approx. 3 hours, after which it is possible to enjoy a free afternoon or take the optional visit to Memphis and Sakkara, the ruins of original Cairo. At night, transfer to the airport to take the flight to Luxor, arrival at Luxor airport, transfer to the cruise ship, accommodation, and dinner on board the cruise.
data.mct001.day9.title: CAIRO - LUXOR

data.mct001.overview: A grand 19-day odyssey spanning four iconic destinations: the mosaic crossroads of İstanbul, the rose-red city of Petra, the timeless pharaonic wonders of Cairo and the Nile, and the dazzling futuristic skyline of Dubai. This carefully curated itinerary blends ancient history, spiritual heritage, desert adventure, and ultra-modern luxury into one seamless, unforgettable journey.
data.mct001.subtitle: 19 Days – Türkiye, Jordan, Egypt & Dubai
data.mct002.day1.desc: Departure on a regular flight to Cairo. Arrival at Cairo airport, visa processing. Assistance by our staff and transfer to the hotel. Accommodation.
data.mct002.day11.desc: Breakfast. At the agreed time, transfer to the airport. End of our services.
data.mct002.day2.desc: Breakfast at the hotel. In the morning, scheduled visit to the site of the Pyramids of Cheops, Chephren, Mykerinus, and the Sphinx. This visit lasts approx. 3 hours. After the visit, departure to the airport to take the domestic flight CAI/ASW. Arrival in Aswan and transfer to the ship. Optionally, departure by road to visit the temples of Abu Simbel, return to Aswan, embarkation on the cruise. Dinner and night on board.
data.mct002.day3.desc: Full board on board. Departure for the excursion to the Philae temple and then navigation towards Kom Ombo. Arrival at sunset to visit the temple of Kom Ombo. Navigation towards Edfu. Night on board.
data.mct002.day4.desc: Full board on board. Departure to visit the Edfu Temple dedicated to the God Horus. Return to the cruise and navigation towards Esna. After passing through the lock, arrival in Luxor. Depending on the ship's navigation schedule, visits could be made this afternoon. Night on board.
data.mct002.day5.desc: Breakfast and check-out at 08:00 hrs. Day dedicated to exploring Luxor: Karnak temple, Luxor temple, Colossi of Memnon, Mortuary Temple of Queen Hatshepsut, and the Valley of the Kings. At the scheduled time, departure to the airport to take the domestic flight LXR/CAI. Arrival in Cairo and transfer to the hotel. Accommodation.
data.mct002.day6.desc: Breakfast and free day. There is an optional scheduled excursion to visit the most emblematic sites of the city: the Citadel of Saladin, Alabaster Mosque, Khan el-Khalili Market, Egyptian Museum, and Coptic Quarter accompanied by a guide and in private transport. Booking is possible at the destination.
data.mct002.day7.desc: Breakfast at the hotel. At the agreed time, transfer to Cairo airport to take the flight to Athens. Arrival at Athens airport and transfer to the chosen hotel. Accommodation.
data.mct002.day8.desc: Breakfast. Departure for the ½ Day City Tour: Acropolis + Panoramic tour of Athens. The Parthenon, a classic symbol of architecture, built entirely of white marble. The Parthenon is the largest temple erected in honor of the goddess Athena. Near the Parthenon is the small Temple of Erechtheion with sculptures of precious maidens holding up the porch roof. The panoramic tour takes us to the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier, guarded by soldiers dressed in traditional attire, the Parliament, and the Panathenaic Stadium, where the first Olympic Games of the modern era were held. Next, we will go towards the Library, the Academy, and the University, three buildings representative of 19th-century architecture blended with classical influence. Free afternoon. Accommodation.
data.mct002.day8.title: ATHENS - ½ Day City Tour
data.mct002.day9.desc: Breakfast. The 1-Day Cruise gives us the opportunity to visit 3 islands of the Saronic Gulf, enjoying the Mediterranean atmosphere, savoring Greek cuisine, accompanied by a traditional and popular program by the ship's orchestra, singers, and dancers. Hydra. The queen of the Saronic islands. Favorite island of artists and the jet-set. Its picturesque port impresses with its unique architecture. Poros. A picturesque island almost attached to the Peloponnese, the smallest of the three, but with exceptional beauty, a green paradise full of lemon and pine trees. Aegina. The largest island of the Saronic Gulf, inhabited since the second millennium BC, with a remarkable historical heritage and a capital full of neoclassical buildings. Return to Athens in the afternoon. Accommodation.
data.mct002.day9.title: ATHENS

data.mct002.overview: A spectacular journey combining two legendary civilizations: from the Pyramids of Giza and the Nile in Egypt to the Acropolis of Athens and the charming Saronic Gulf islands in Greece. A unique 11-day experience encompassing history, culture, and unforgettable landscapes.

data.mct003.day1.desc: Arrival and visa processing, transfer to the hotel and accommodation.
data.mct003.day1.title: THE CAIRO
data.mct003.day10.desc: Breakfast at the hotel. Visit to this fascinating region and original landscape, formed by the lava thrown by the Erciyes and Hasan volcanoes 3 million years ago. Visit to the open-air museum of the Goreme valley, an incredible Byzantine monastic complex made up of churches carved into the rock with beautiful frescoes. Visit to the Avcilar and Güvercinlik valleys, which have a spectacular landscape of the so-called Fairy Chimneys, lunch during the visit in a local restaurant. After lunch, visit to the Pasabag valley and panoramic views of the Cappadocia valleys to take the best photos of Cappadocia. Continuation with typical visits to carpet, onyx, and turquoise workshops where quality and a good price can be found. Dinner at the hotel. Accommodation at a hotel in Cappadocia. (During your stay in Cappadocia, you will have the possibility to participate in a folklore dance show in a typical cave with unlimited regional drinks and take an optional hot air balloon excursion at dawn).
data.mct003.day10.title: CAPPADOCIA (FULL BOARD)
data.mct003.day11.desc: Breakfast and departure for Konya. Arrival and visit to the Silk Inn (Caravanserai). Lunch in a local restaurant and then departure to Pamukkale, a place where the high calcium concentration of its waters has created one of the most spectacular formations in the world. Dinner and accommodation at the hotel. Weather permitting, they will have free time at the hotel to enjoy the thermal baths of the Hotel.
data.mct003.day11.title: CAPPADOCIA – KONYA – PAMUKKALE (FULL BOARD)
data.mct003.day12.desc: Breakfast at the hotel. Visit to Hierapolis, an ancient Hellenistic city that today is in ruins. Visit to the famous cotton castle, a natural wonder of gigantic white waterfalls, stalactites, and natural pools formed over the centuries by the passage of waters loaded with calcium salts from thermal springs. Transfer by bus to Selcuk, center of Ephesus. Continuation to Ephesus, the best-preserved ancient city in Asia Minor; during the 1st and 2nd centuries, it had a population of 250,000 inhabitants and monopolized the wealth of the Middle East. We will visit the Temple of Hadrian, the Roman Baths, the Library, the Odeon, the Theater, etc. Continuation with a visit to the HOUSE OF THE VIRGIN MARY, and in the afternoon, a visit to a leather workshop where we can see the famous leather goods of the Aegean region. Lunch in a local restaurant en route. Transfer by bus to the Izmir Zone. Dinner at the hotel. Accommodation at a hotel in the Izmir Zone.
data.mct003.day12.title: PAMUKKALE – IZMIR ZONE (FULL BOARD)
data.mct003.day13.desc: Breakfast at the hotel. Transfer by bus to Bursa. Arrival in Bursa and visit to the Green Mosque or Yesil Camii, built in 1419 by Mehmet I Çelebi. Next to the Mosque, we find the Green Mausoleum. It may be the most beautiful building in all of Bursa. Transfer by bus to Istanbul. Accommodation at a hotel in Istanbul.
data.mct003.day13.title: IZMIR ZONE – BURSA - ISTANBUL (BED & BREAKFAST)
data.mct003.day14.desc: Breakfast at the hotel. Departure in the morning to visit the Sehzade Mosque (Sehzade Camii, in Turkish), which is one of the most outstanding monuments in Istanbul, as it can be considered the first representation of classical Ottoman architecture. Next, we will experience one of the most famous activities in Istanbul, a boat trip along the Bosphorus, the channel that separates Europe and Asia. During this journey, you can appreciate the palaces of the Sultans, old and typical wooden houses, and enjoy history in a different way. We will culminate our day with one of the star visits, the Spice Bazaar, established by the Ottomans 5 centuries ago and used ever since. We will enjoy free time. End of the visit at the Egyptian Bazaar.
data.mct003.day14.title: ISTANBUL (BED & BREAKFAST)
data.mct003.day15.desc: Breakfast. Transfer to Istanbul airport. End of our services.
data.mct003.day15.title: ISTANBUL - DESTINATION
data.mct003.day2.desc: Breakfast and check-out at 12:00 hrs, in the morning scheduled visit to the site of the Pyramids of Cheops, Chephren, Mykerinus, and the Sphinx. This visit lasts approx. 3 hours, after which it is possible to enjoy a free afternoon or take the optional visit to Memphis and Sakkara, the ruins of original Cairo. At night, flight to Luxor, arrival and transfer to the Ship, cold dinner in the room and night on board.
data.mct003.day2.title: THE CAIRO – EXCURSION OF THE PYRAMIDS OF GIZA – FLIGHT TO LUXOR – EMBARKATION
data.mct003.day3.desc: Full board. At the scheduled time, the visit to the eastern bank of Luxor will begin, with the Karnak Temple, which dominates the entire site of Thebes born from the splendor of the pharaohs of the New Kingdom, and the Luxor Temple, the work of two great pharaohs, Ramesses II and Ramesses III. Continuation of the visit to the western bank with the Necropolis of Thebes, including the Valley of the Kings where the tombs of the most important pharaohs of the New Kingdom are hidden, the Mortuary Temple of Queen Hatshepsut, and the giant and grandiose Colossi of Memnon. Return to the cruise ship, embarkation, and navigation towards Edfu. Night on board.
data.mct003.day3.title: NILE CRUISE (FULL BOARD)
data.mct003.day4.desc: Full board. At the scheduled time, visit to the Edfu Temple dedicated to the God Horus. Return to the cruise ship and navigation towards Kom Ombo. Arrival and visit to its Temple dedicated to the gods Sobek and Haroeris. Navigation towards Aswan. Night on board.
data.mct003.day4.title: NILE CRUISE (FULL BOARD)
data.mct003.day5.desc: Full board. Departure to visit the Philae Temple and a ride in a felucca (typical fishing boats) along the Nile, contemplating several volcanic islands of black granite. If you wish to take the optional visit to Abu Simbel, it is scheduled for this day. Night on board.
data.mct003.day5.title: NILE CRUISE (FULL BOARD)
data.mct003.day6.desc: After breakfast, disembarkation and check-out from the ship's cabins at 08:00 hrs, free time in Aswan. At the scheduled time, transfer to Aswan airport to take the flight to Cairo, transfer to the hotel, and the rest of the day free. Accommodation.
data.mct003.day6.title: DISEMBARKATION - FLIGHT BOUND FOR CAIRO (BREAKFAST)
data.mct003.day7.desc: Breakfast and free day. There is an optional scheduled excursion to visit the most emblematic sites of the city: The Citadel of Saladin, Alabaster Mosque, Khan el-Khalili Market, Egyptian Museum, and Coptic Quarter accompanied by a guide and in private transport. Booking is possible at the destination.
data.mct003.day7.title: CAIRO
data.mct003.day8.desc: Breakfast and check-out, and at the scheduled time, transfer to the airport to take the flight to Istanbul, arrival and transfer to the hotel, accommodation.
data.mct003.day8.title: THE CAIRO - ISTANBUL
data.mct003.day9.desc: Breakfast and departure in the morning to Ankara, passing through the Bolu mountains. In Ankara, visit to the Mausoleum of Ataturk. In 1953, this Mausoleum was inaugurated dedicated to the founder of modern Turkey, Ataturk. It is a marble block with 40-ton columns, reached by a long street with lions on its sides, ending in an immense square. Lunch in a local restaurant and then continuation towards Cappadocia. On the route, we will also visit the salt lake. Arrival, dinner, and accommodation at the hotel.
data.mct003.day9.title: ISTANBUL - ANKARA – CAPPADOCIA (FULL BOARD)

data.mct004.day1.desc: Arrival at Dubai International Airport, reception by a Spanish-speaking assistant and transfer to the hotel.
data.mct004.day1.title: INTERNATIONAL FLIGHT DUBAI
data.mct004.day10.desc: Breakfast at the hotel. Departure to Hierapolis, an ancient Hellenistic city that today is in ruins. Visit to the famous cotton castle, a natural wonder of gigantic white waterfalls, stalactites, and natural pools formed over the centuries by the passage of waters loaded with calcium salts from thermal springs. Transfer by bus to Selcuk, center of Ephesus Excursion, the best-preserved ancient city in Asia Minor; during the 1st and 2nd centuries, it had a population of 250,000 inhabitants and monopolized the wealth of the Middle East. We will visit the Temple of Hadrian, the Roman Baths, the Library, the Odeon, the Theater, etc. Continuation with a visit to the HOUSE OF THE VIRGIN MARY, and in the afternoon, a visit to a leather workshop where we can see the famous leather goods of the Aegean region. Lunch in a local restaurant en route. Transfer by bus to the Izmir Zone. Dinner at the hotel. Accommodation at a hotel in the Izmir Zone.
data.mct004.day10.title: PAMUKKALE – EPHESUS – IZMIR ZONE.
data.mct004.day11.desc: Breakfast at the hotel. Transfer by bus to Bursa. Arrival in Bursa and visit to the Green Mosque or Yesil Camii, built in 1419 by Mehmet I Çelebi. Next to the Mosque, we find the Green Mausoleum. It may be the most beautiful building in all of Bursa. Transfer by bus to Istanbul. Accommodation at a hotel in Istanbul.
data.mct004.day11.title: IZMIR ZONE – BURSA – ISTANBUL.
data.mct004.day12.desc: Breakfast at the hotel. Departure in the morning to visit the Sehzade Mosque (Sehzade Camii, in Turkish), which is one of the most outstanding monuments in Istanbul, as it can be considered the first representation of classical Ottoman architecture. Next, we will experience one of the most famous activities in Istanbul, a boat trip along the Bosphorus, the channel that separates Europe and Asia. During this journey, you can appreciate the palaces of the Sultans, old and typical wooden houses, and enjoy history in a different way. We will culminate our day with one of the star visits, the Spice Bazaar, established by the Ottomans 5 centuries ago and used ever since. We will enjoy free time. End of the visit at the Egyptian Bazaar.
data.mct004.day12.title: ISTANBUL
data.mct004.day13.desc: Breakfast at the hotel. Free day. Free time to explore the city with the possibility of taking an optional full-day excursion "The Jewels of Constantinople" with lunch: visit to the ancient Hippodrome belonging to the Byzantine era, which was the center of civil activity in the country and where not only horse races and gladiator fights took place, but also celebrations in honor of the Emperor. Today it preserves the Obelisk of Theodosius, the Serpentine Column, and the Fountain of Emperor Wilhelm. Blue Mosque, built in 1609 during the sultanate of Ahmet, who, wanting to surpass the Hagia Sophia Basilica with his mosque, built it with 6 minarets. Next, we will go to the Topkapi Palace, residence of the sultans until the 19th century, where most of the valuable objects of the Ottoman sultans are kept. The visit to Hagia Sophia, the most finished example of Byzantine art and one of humanity's greatest achievements. Next, transfer for the visit to the Grand Bazaar. Covered bazaar built in the year 1660 which can be considered as a small city covered by hundreds of domes, four thousand shops inside with alleys, squares, and 18 access gates. End of the visit at the Grand Bazaar.
data.mct004.day13.title: ISTANBUL
data.mct004.day14.desc: Breakfast at the hotel. At the scheduled time, transfer to the airport. End of services.
data.mct004.day14.title: ISTANBUL - CITY OF ORIGIN
data.mct004.day2.desc: Breakfast at the hotel. And half-day visit. Dubai is a city famous for its modernity, but it is important to know its history. This day we will have the opportunity to stroll through the old part of the city. Our first stop will be in the Bastakiya area; in this open-air museum, we can admire the small streets and their old Arabic-style houses with wind towers on each of them. We will enter one of the houses open to the public and learn a little about the customs and cultures of the local people. We will cross in an abra (handcrafted boat) to the spice and gold market. We will lose ourselves in the small streets enjoying the aroma and flavors to find the perfect souvenir to take home. In the shops of the gold market, you can find real jewelry in gold, platinum, and silver.
data.mct004.day2.title: CLASSIC DUBAI VISIT
data.mct004.day3.desc: Breakfast and visit to Abu Dhabi. This visit takes you to the capital Abu Dhabi, which is approximately 1 hour and 30 minutes from Dubai. On the way, we will pass by the Jebel Ali Port, which is the largest artificial port in the world. Upon arrival in the capital, our first stop will be at one of the largest mosques in the world. The Sheikh Zayed Mosque is a true architectural masterpiece, open to visitors from all over the world and of all religions. As we continue, we will take a panoramic tour passing by the palaces of the royal family of Abu Dhabi. Then a photo stop at the Emirates Palace and the Presidential Palace (from the outside). We continue along the beautiful Corniche (the promenade); we will make a stop at the cultural island of Abu Dhabi to photograph the Louvre Museum, opened in 2018. To conclude, we will tour Yas Island and make a stop to take photos at Ferrari World. Transfer to Dubai.
data.mct004.day3.title: DUBAI - ABU DHABI
data.mct004.day4.desc: Breakfast at the hotel and free morning. At 15:00, we will leave in modern 4x4 cars into the Dubai desert. Our English-speaking driver will take us on this adventure through the sand dunes; we will enjoy the most famous traditional ride of all. Women can wear brightly colored clothes and dresses to take photos at sunset; the best photos of couples and families can also be taken in this place. After sunset, our driver takes us to the camp where a delicious oriental buffet with a variety of dishes awaits us. During dinner, we will enjoy the oriental show, belly dancing, tanoura show, and also a fire show. At the end of the dinner, transfer back to the hotel.
data.mct004.day4.title: DUBAI SAFARI 4 x 4.
data.mct004.day5.desc: Breakfast at the hotel, free day in Dubai to make optional visits.
data.mct004.day5.title: DUBAI
data.mct004.day6.desc: Breakfast at the hotel. Departure to Istanbul International Airport with a Spanish-speaking assistant and transfer to the hotel. Arrival in Istanbul. Accommodation.
data.mct004.day6.title: DUBAI/ISTANBUL
data.mct004.day7.desc: Breakfast and departure in the morning to Ankara, passing through the Bolu mountains. In Ankara, visit to the Mausoleum of Ataturk. In 1953, this Mausoleum was inaugurated dedicated to the founder of modern Turkey, Ataturk. It is a marble block with 40-ton columns, reached by a long street with lions on its sides, ending in an immense square. Lunch in a local restaurant and then continuation towards Cappadocia. On the route, we will also visit the salt lake. Arrival, dinner, and accommodation at the hotel. (Optional: A domestic flight can be booked)
data.mct004.day7.title: ISTANBUL – ANKARA – CAPPADOCIA
data.mct004.day8.desc: Breakfast at the hotel. Visit to this fascinating region and original landscape, formed by the lava thrown by the Erciyes and Hasan volcanoes 3 million years ago. Visit to the open-air museum of the Goreme valley, an incredible Byzantine monastic complex made up of churches carved into the rock with beautiful frescoes. Visit to the Avcilar and Güvercinlik valleys, which have a spectacular landscape of the so-called Fairy Chimneys, lunch during the visit in a local restaurant. We will continue with the visit to the Pasabag or Cavusin Valley. During the excursion, we will have the opportunity to visit typical workshops of carpets, onyx, and turquoise where good quality can be found at the best price. Dinner at the hotel. Accommodation at a hotel in Cappadocia. (During your stay in Cappadocia, you will have the possibility to participate in a folklore dance show in a typical cave with unlimited regional drinks and take an optional hot air balloon excursion at dawn).
data.mct004.day8.title: CAPPADOCIA.
data.mct004.day9.desc: Breakfast and departure for Pamukkale. Along the way, we pass through the Konya plain, and visit a caravanserai, which is an ancient type of building that arose along the main roads where caravans making long journeys of many days —for commerce, pilgrimage, or military purposes— used to rest. Lunch in a local restaurant and then continuation to Pamukkale, a place where the high calcium concentration of its waters has created one of the most spectacular formations in the world. Dinner and accommodation at the hotel. Weather permitting, they will have free time at the hotel to enjoy the thermal baths of the Hotel.
data.mct004.day9.title: CAPPADOCIA – KONYA – PAMUKKALE
