data.mct004.title: Marvels of Dubai and Turkey
data.mct005.day1.desc: Breakfast. Transfer to the airport (FLIGHT NOT INCLUDED) bound for Amman, arrival and transfer to the hotel, accommodation.
data.mct005.day1.title: INTERNATIONAL ARRIVAL - AMMAN
data.mct005.day10.desc: After breakfast, disembarkation and check-out from the ship's cabins at 08:00 hrs, free time in Aswan. At the scheduled time, transfer to Aswan airport to take the flight to Cairo, transfer to the hotel and the rest of the day free. Accommodation. There is an optional scheduled excursion to visit the most emblematic sites of the city: The Citadel of Saladin, Alabaster Mosque, Khan el-Khalili Market, Egyptian Museum, and Coptic Quarter accompanied by a guide and in private transport. Booking is possible at the destination.
data.mct005.day10.title: DISEMBARKATION - FLIGHT TO CAIRO
data.mct005.day11.desc: Breakfast and check-out and at the scheduled time, transfer to the airport to take the flight bound for Spain. End of our services. Arrival at Dubai International Airport. At this place, where thousands of passengers arrive each year, our representative will be waiting for you. Transfer to the chosen hotel and accommodation.
data.mct005.day11.title: THE CAIRO - DUBAI
data.mct005.day12.desc: Breakfast at the hotel. And half-day visit. Dubai is a city famous for its modernity, but it is important to know its history. This day we will have the opportunity to stroll through the old part of the city. Our first stop will be in the Bastakiya area; in this open-air museum, we can admire the small streets and their old Arabic-style houses with wind towers on each of them. We will enter one of the houses open to the public and learn a little about the customs and cultures of the local people. We will cross in an abra (handcrafted boat) to the spice and gold market. We will lose ourselves in the small streets enjoying the aroma and flavors to find the perfect souvenir to take home. In the shops of the gold market, you can find real jewelry in gold, platinum, and silver.
data.mct005.day12.title: DUBAI
data.mct005.day13.desc: Breakfast and visit to Abu Dhabi. This visit takes you to the capital Abu Dhabi, which is approximately 1 hour and 30 minutes from Dubai. On the way, we will pass by the Jebel Ali Port, which is the largest artificial port in the world. Upon arrival in the capital, our first stop will be at one of the largest mosques in the world. The Sheikh Zayed Mosque is a true architectural masterpiece, open to visitors from all over the world and of all religions. As we continue, we will take a panoramic tour passing by the palaces of the royal family of Abu Dhabi. Then a photo stop at the Emirates Palace and the Presidential Palace (from the outside). We continue along the beautiful Corniche (the promenade); we will make a stop at the cultural island of Abu Dhabi to photograph the Louvre Museum, opened in 2018. To conclude, we will tour Yas Island and make a stop to take photos at Ferrari World. Transfer to Dubai.
data.mct005.day13.title: DUBAI - ABU DHABI
data.mct005.day14.desc: Breakfast at the hotel and free morning. At 15:00, we will leave in modern 4x4 cars into the Dubai desert. Our English-speaking driver will take us on this adventure through the sand dunes; we will enjoy the most famous traditional ride of all. Women can wear brightly colored clothes and dresses to take photos at sunset; the best photos of couples and families can also be taken in this place. After sunset, our driver takes us to the camp where a delicious oriental buffet with a variety of dishes awaits us. During dinner, we will enjoy the oriental show, belly dancing, tanoura show, and also a fire show. At the end of the dinner, transfer back to the hotel.
data.mct005.day14.title: DUBAI
data.mct005.day15.desc: Breakfast at the hotel, free day in Dubai to make optional visits.
data.mct005.day15.title: ABU DHABI - DUBAI
data.mct005.day16.desc: Breakfast at the hotel. According to your flight schedule, we will leave the hotel 3 and a half hours before your flight departure time.
data.mct005.day16.title: DUBAI - DESTINATION
data.mct005.day2.desc: Breakfast and departure to Madaba. Arrival and visit to the Church of Saint George, with its famous mosaic representing all the biblical territories. Continuation to Mount Nebo, from whose summit Moses glimpsed the Promised Land. Visit to the mosaic collection. Transfer for the visit to Shoubak Castle, sometimes referred to as "Mont Real", Shoubak dates back to the same turbulent period as Karak. It is perched on the side of a mountain, over a wide area of fruit trees. The exterior of Shoubak Castle is impressive, with an imposing gate and a triple wall around it. Despite the precautions taken by its builder, the fortress fell into Saladin's hands only 75 years after its construction. The inscriptions of its proud successors appear on the castle walls. Continuation to Petra. Arrival in Petra. Dinner and accommodation at the hotel.
data.mct005.day2.title: AMMAN - MADABA - MOUNT NEBO - SHOUBAK CASTLE - PETRA
data.mct005.day3.desc: Breakfast. Day entirely dedicated to the visit of Petra, known as the "rose city", where more than 2,000 years ago the Nabataeans located the capital of their empire for over 500 years, carving admirable temples and tombs into the pink mountains and using advanced agricultural and water conduction systems. The tour begins with the Tomb of the Obelisks, continuing through the Siq, a canyon more than 1 km long after which the Treasury is discovered, a colossal tomb decorated with columns and sculptures of incomparable refinement and beauty. Continuation towards the street of facades and the theater to approach the 850 steps that will lead us to the imposing Monasterio "El Deir". Dinner and accommodation at the hotel.
data.mct005.day3.title: PETRA
data.mct005.day4.desc: Breakfast. Visit Little Petra, which was inhabited by the Nabataeans and has many tombs, water containers, and channels; it has a small path leading to some of the inner area, Siq El Bared. The scale of this area and the fact that it is the continuation of Petra gave it the name of Little Petra. Departure towards Wadi Rum. Excursion to the Wadi Rum desert and ride in 4x4 vehicles. We will enter the pink sands of this desert, which possesses a special charm provided by the granite massifs that nature has modeled into whimsical shapes. Return to Amman. Dinner and accommodation.
data.mct005.day4.title: PETRA - LITTLE PETRA - WADI RUM - AMMAN - CAIRO
data.mct005.day5.desc: Arrival at Cairo airport, reception by our representatives, visa processing, baggage collection, transfer to the hotel, accommodation and night in Cairo.
data.mct005.day5.title: THE CAIRO
data.mct005.day6.desc: Breakfast, in the morning scheduled visit to the site of the Pyramids of Cheops, Chephren, Mykerinus, and the Sphinx. This visit lasts approx. 3 hours, after which it is possible to enjoy a free afternoon or take the optional visit to Memphis and Sakkara, the ruins of original Cairo. At night, transfer to the airport to take the flight to Luxor, arrival at Luxor airport, transfer to the cruise ship, accommodation and dinner on board the cruise.
data.mct005.day6.title: THE CAIRO - LUXOR
data.mct005.day7.desc: Full board. At the scheduled time, the visit to the eastern bank of Luxor will begin, with the Karnak Temple, which dominates the entire site of Thebes born from the splendor of the pharaohs of the New Kingdom, and the Luxor Temple, the work of two great pharaohs, Ramesses II and Ramesses III. Continuation of the visit to the western bank with the Necropolis of Thebes, including the Valley of the Kings where the tombs of the most important pharaohs of the New Kingdom are hidden, the Mortuary Temple of Queen Hatshepsut, and the giant and grandiose Colossi of Memnon. Return to the cruise ship, embarkation, and navigation towards Edfu. Night on board.
data.mct005.day7.title: NILE CRUISE (FULL BOARD)
data.mct005.day8.desc: Full board. At the scheduled time, visit to the Edfu Temple dedicated to the God Horus. Return to the cruise ship and navigation towards Kom Ombo. Arrival and visit to its Temple dedicated to the gods Sobek and Haroeris. Navigation towards Aswan. Night on board.
data.mct005.day8.title: NILE CRUISE (FULL BOARD)
data.mct005.day9.desc: Full board. Departure to visit the Philae Temple and a ride in a felucca (typical fishing boats) along the Nile, contemplating several volcanic islands of black granite. If you wish to take the optional visit to Abu Simbel, it is scheduled for this day. Night on board.
data.mct005.day9.title: NILE CRUISE (FULL BOARD)

data.mct005.overview: NET PRICES IN USD: Option 4/5****. Embark on an extraordinary 16-day journey through three of the Middle East's most captivating destinations. Begin in Jordan, exploring the ancient Nabataean city of Petra, the vast desert of Wadi Rum, and historical sites like Mount Nebo and Madaba. Continue to Egypt for a magical Nile cruise between Luxor and Aswan, visiting the iconic Pyramids of Giza, the majestic temples of Karnak and Luxor, and the Valley of the Kings. Finally, conclude your adventure in Dubai, the city of superlatives.
data.mct005.subtitle: 03 Jordan + 07 Nights Egypt + 05 Dubai
data.mct005.title: Stars of the Middle East 16 Days

data.mct006.day2.desc: Breakfast. Departure to El Jem, visit to its Roman amphitheater, the largest in Africa, which competes even with the one in Rome due to its large dimensions. Continuation to Matmata. Arrival and Lunch. Visit to a troglodyte house (typical underground house of the Berber peoples). Continuation to Douz, known as "The Gateway to the Desert". In the afternoon, possibility of taking optional excursions through the Saharan dunes such as a ride on the back of a dromedary, and Quads (Optional). Dinner and accommodation at a 4* Hotel.
data.mct006.day2.title: TUNIS - EL JEM - MATMATA - DOUZ
data.mct006.day3.desc: Breakfast, departure to Tozeur, capital of "El Jerid", the region of palm trees, passing through Chott El Jerid, a spectacular salt lake known for its mirages and located just 25 meters above sea level. Stop to take photos of the water springs. Continuation to Tozeur. Arrival at Hotel Ksar Rouge 4*. Lunch. In the afternoon, possibility of taking the 4x4 excursion to the mountain oases of Chebika, Tamerza, and Ong Ejmel (Optional). Return to the hotel. Dinner and accommodation.
data.mct006.day3.title: DOUZ - CHOTT EL JERID - TOZEUR - CHEBIKA - ONG JMEL – TOZEUR
data.mct006.day4.desc: Breakfast. Departure to Kairouan, the fourth holy city of Islam, visit to the Grand Mosque, the Mausoleum of the Barber, and the Aghlabid pools. Lunch. Afterwards, visit to a carpet cooperative. Next, departure to Hammamet. Arrival at hotel MEHARI HAMMAMET 5* on All Inclusive (TI) basis.
data.mct006.day4.title: TOZEUR - KAIROUAN - HAMMAMET BEACH ZONE
data.mct006.day5.desc: Free days to enjoy the hotel and the beach on All Inclusive (TI) basis. You can also take an optional excursion to Sidi Bou Said, Carthage, and Tunis.

data.mct006.overview: NET PRICES IN USD: Option 4/5****. Embark on an extraordinary 16-day journey through the treasures of Tunisia and Egypt. Discover Tunisia's diverse landscapes from the Roman amphitheater of El Jem and the troglodyte homes of Matmata to the golden Sahara dunes of Douz, the spectacular salt lake of Chott El Jerid, and the mountain oases of Chebika. Relax on the beautiful beaches of Hammamet, then continue to Egypt for a magical Nile cruise between Luxor and Aswan, visiting the iconic Pyramids of Giza, the majestic temples of Karnak and Luxor, the Valley of the Kings, and the Temple of Philae.

data.mct007.overview: Embark on a 13-day journey combining the modern marvels of Dubai with the ancient wonders of Egypt. Explore Dubai's historic Bastakiya district, cross Dubai Creek by abra, visit the magnificent Sheikh Zayed Mosque in Abu Dhabi, and experience an unforgettable desert safari with BBQ dinner. Continue to Egypt to discover the iconic Pyramids of Giza and the Sphinx, then enjoy a magical Nile cruise between Luxor and Aswan, visiting the Valley of the Kings, the Temple of Queen Hatshepsut, and the majestic temples of Karnak, Luxor, Edfu, and Kom Ombo.
data.mct007.subtitle: 05 NIGHTS DUBAI + 07 NIGHTS EGYPT
data.mct007.title: Egypt and Dubai 13 Days
data.mct008.day1.desc: Transfer from the different pickup points of Costa del Sol towards Tarifa. Embarkation on the ferry from Tarifa towards Tangier. Upon arrival, our Moroccan guide awaits us and accompanies us throughout the tour. Departure towards the imperial city of Meknes, to make a panoramic visit of the city and the walls of Bab el Mansour. Continuation towards Fez. Arrival at the end of the afternoon. Dinner and accommodation at the hotel.
data.mct008.day1.title: DAY 1: COSTA DEL SOL / TARIFA / TANGIER / MEKNES / FEZ (160 Kms. Spain + 399 kms. Morocco)
data.mct008.day10.desc: After breakfast, disembarkation and check-out from the ship's cabins at 08:00 hrs, free time in Aswan. At the scheduled time, transfer to Aswan airport to take the flight bound for Cairo, transfer to the hotel and the rest of the day free. Accommodation.
data.mct008.day10.title: DISEMBARKATION - FLIGHT BOUND FOR CAIRO (BREAKFAST AND LUNCH)
data.mct008.day11.desc: Breakfast and free day. There is an optional scheduled excursion to visit the most emblematic sites of the city: The Citadel of Saladin, Alabaster Mosque, Khan el-Khalili Market, Egyptian Museum, and Coptic Quarter accompanied by a guide. Booking is possible at the destination.
data.mct008.day11.title: THE CAIRO
data.mct008.day12.desc: Breakfast and check-out and at the scheduled time, transfer to the airport to take the flight bound for Spain. End of our services.
data.mct008.day12.title: THE CAIRO – CITY OF ORIGIN
data.mct008.day2.desc: Breakfast. Complete visit of the city considered the religious capital of the country. Founded in the 8th century by Idriss II, it is considered one of the 4 imperial capitals of Morocco. During this visit, a tour is made through the most emblematic places such as the exteriors of the Royal Palace, the Jewish quarter, and the fortress that has the best view of Fez el Bali, the medina or old city. A walking tour is made for several hours, entering the medina through the famous gate of Bab Bou Jeloud and having the possibility to experience a return to the past in a part of the city that still lives close to the Middle Ages. Walls protect its interior, formed by hundreds of labyrinthinely organized alleys where clients can experience a thousand smells and flavors in this place considered a World Heritage Site. Dinner and accommodation at the hotel.
data.mct008.day2.title: DAY 2: FEZ
data.mct008.day3.desc: Breakfast. Early departure towards Rabat, capital of the country, to visit the most emblematic places of this city such as the exteriors of the royal palace, the Mausoleum of Mohamed V, and the Hassan tower. Continuation towards Casablanca to visit the most emblematic places of this city such as the United Nations square, the Boulevard de la Corniche, and the exteriors of the Hassan II mosque. Once the visit is finished, free lunch on route and continuation towards Marrakech. Accommodation at the hotel.
data.mct008.day3.title: DAY 3: FEZ / RABAT / CASABLANCA / MARRAKECH (532 kms)
data.mct008.day4.desc: Breakfast. Visit of the city called "Pearl of the South", which begins in the grandiose Menara Gardens, which feature a side pavilion and countless olive trees. Next, exterior visit of the Koutoubia minaret, twin sister of the Giralda in Seville. Once inside the old part of the city, a visit is made to the Bahia Palace, property of a city noble. Finally, arrival at Jemaa el Fna square, one of the most interesting places in Morocco from where the souks and the medina are accessed. Guilds of wood artisans, ceramics, furriers, or spice merchants will be some of the places that will be visited. Once the visit is finished, lunch at a hotel in Marrakech and subsequent departure towards Casablanca. Arrival in Casablanca. Dinner and accommodation at the hotel.
data.mct008.day4.title: DAY 4: MARRAKECH / CASABLANCA (243 kms)
data.mct008.day5.desc: Breakfast. Departure towards Tangier. Arrival in Tangier and transfer to the port for departure on the ferry towards Tarifa. Continuation towards the Costa del Sol., at the scheduled time transfer to the airport to take the flight towards Cairo. Transfer to the hotel, accommodation.
data.mct008.day5.title: DAY 5: CASABLANCA / TANGIER / TARIFA / COSTA DEL SOL - CAIRO
data.mct008.day6.desc: After breakfast, scheduled visit to the site of the Pyramids of Cheops, Chephren, Mykerinus, and the Sphinx. This visit lasts approx. 3 hours, after which it is possible to enjoy a free afternoon or take the optional visit to Memphis and Sakkara, the ruins of original Cairo, then we head to the airport to take the flight bound for Luxor, arrival, reception by our staff and transfer to the motonave on the banks of the Nile. Dinner on board (depending on the arrival time, it may be offered as a buffet or already prepared).
data.mct008.day6.title: DAY 6: CAIRO – LUXOR
data.mct008.day7.desc: Full board. At the scheduled time, the visit to the eastern bank of Luxor will begin, with the Karnak Temple, which dominates the entire site of Thebes born from the splendor of the pharaohs of the New Kingdom, and the Luxor Temple, the work of two great pharaohs, Ramesses II and Ramesses III. Continuation of the visit to the western bank with the Necropolis of Thebes, including the Valley of the Kings where the tombs of the most important pharaohs of the New Kingdom are hidden, the Mortuary Temple of Queen Hatshepsut, and the giant and grandiose Colossi of Memnon. Return to the cruise ship, embarkation, and navigation towards Edfu. Night on board.
data.mct008.day7.title: DAY 7: NILE CRUISE (FULL BOARD - PC)
data.mct008.day8.desc: Full board. At the scheduled time, visit to the Edfu Temple dedicated to the God Horus. Return to the cruise ship and navigation towards Kom Ombo. Arrival and visit to its Temple dedicated to the gods Sobek and Haroeris. Navigation towards Aswan. Night on board.
data.mct008.day8.title: DAY 8: NILE CRUISE (FULL BOARD - PC)
data.mct008.day9.desc: Full board. Departure to visit the Philae Temple and a ride in a felucca (typical fishing boats) along the Nile, contemplating several volcanic islands of black granite. It is possible to take the optional visit to Abu Simbel, it is scheduled for this day. Night on board.
data.mct008.day9.title: DAY 9: NILE CRUISE (FULL BOARD - PC)

data.mct008.egypt.excl.2: Travel tips and assistance: 45 euro per adult or child, mandatory payment at destination (guide tips not included).

data.mct008.egypt.incl.2: 3 nights Cairo on a bed and breakfast basis.
data.mct008.egypt.incl.3: Visits, entrance fees, and transfers according to the itinerary.
data.mct008.highlight.1: Explore the imperial cities of Morocco: Meknes, Fez, Rabat, and Marrakech
data.mct008.highlight.2: Stroll through the labyrinthine alleys of Fez el Bali, a UNESCO World Heritage site
data.mct008.highlight.3: Visit the Hassan II Mosque in Casablanca and the Mausoleum of Mohamed V in Rabat
data.mct008.highlight.4: Discover the Pyramids of Giza, the Sphinx, and fly to Luxor
data.mct008.highlight.5: Enjoy a Nile cruise visiting the Valley of the Kings, Karnak, Luxor, Edfu, Kom Ombo, and Philae temples
data.mct008.morocco.incl.1: Transfers Costa del Sol / Tarifa / Costa del Sol.
data.mct008.morocco.incl.4: Escort guide on the tour in Morocco.
data.mct008.morocco.incl.5: 4 hotel nights on a half board basis (3 dinners + 1 lunch).
data.mct008.overview: Embark on a 13-day journey through the vibrant spices, rich history, and breathtaking landscapes of Morocco and Egypt. Explore the imperial cities of Meknes, Fez, Rabat, and Marrakech. Cross the Strait of Gibraltar by ferry, then continue to Egypt to discover the Pyramids of Giza and enjoy a magical Nile cruise from Luxor to Aswan, visiting the Valley of the Kings, the temples of Karnak, Luxor, Edfu, Kom Ombo, and Philae.
data.mct008.subtitle: 05 days Morocco / 08 days Egypt
data.mct008.title: Spices of Egypt and Morocco
data.mct009.day1.desc: Presentation at the airport 2 hours before the departure of your flight, check-in and boarding, arrival in Amman. Transfer to the hotel. Accommodation. (Dinner would not be included if arrival at the hotel is after 21:00 hrs)
data.mct009.day1.title: DAY 1: FLIGHTS FROM CITY OF ORIGIN – AMMAN
data.mct009.day10.desc: Breakfast. In the morning, planned visit to the complex of the Pyramids of Cheops, Chephren, Mycerinus and the Sphinx. This visit lasts approximately 3 hours. After completing it, it is possible to enjoy a free afternoon or take optional paid excursions at the destination such as the Cairo Museum, etc.
data.mct009.day10.title: DAY 10: CAIRO
data.mct009.day11.desc: Breakfast and at the scheduled time transfer to the airport to take the flight to Spain. End of our services.
data.mct009.day11.title: DAY 11: CAIRO – MADRID OR BARCELONA
data.mct009.day2.desc: Breakfast and departure to visit the Orthodox Church of Saint George, where the first mosaic map of Palestine is found. Continuation towards Mount Nebo to admire the panoramic view of the Jordan Valley and the Dead Sea from the mountain. This place is important because it was the last place visited by Moses and from where the prophet glimpsed the Promised Land, which he would never reach. Transfer for the visit of Shobak Castle, sometimes called 'Mont Real', Shobak dates from the same turbulent period as Karak. It is nestled on the hillside of a mountain, above a wide area of fruit trees. The exterior of Shobak Castle is impressive, with an imposing gate and a triple wall surrounding it. Despite the precautions taken by its builder, the fortress fell into Saladin's hands only 75 years after its construction. The inscriptions of its proud successors appear on the walls of the castle. Continuation to Petra. Dinner and accommodation.
data.mct009.day2.title: DAY 2: AMMAN – MADABA – NEBO – SHOBAK – PETRA
data.mct009.day3.desc: Full day dedicated to the visit of the rose city, the capital of the Nabataeans. During the visit, we will get to know the most important and representative monuments carved in the rock by the Nabataeans. The Treasury, a famous and internationally known monument brought to the cinema in one of the Indiana Jones films, the Coloured Tombs, the Royal Tombs, the Monastery... Petra is one of those places in the world where you have to go at least once in your lifetime. At the end of the visit, already in the afternoon, return to the hotel. Dinner and accommodation.
data.mct009.day3.title: DAY 3: PETRA
data.mct009.day4.desc: Breakfast. Visit Little Petra (Pequeña Petra) was inhabited by the Nabataeans and has many tombs, water containers and channels, it has a small path that leads to some of the interior area, Siq Al-Bared, the scale of this area and the fact that it is the continuation of Petra, gave it the name of Little Petra. Departure towards Wadi Rum. Excursion to the Wadi Rum desert and ride in 4x4 vehicles. We will venture into the pink sands of this desert, which has a special charm provided by the granite massifs that nature has shaped with whimsical forms. Dinner and accommodation at the Camp.
data.mct009.day4.title: DAY 4: PETRA – LITTLE PETRA – WADI RUM
data.mct009.day5.desc: Breakfast. Continuation towards the airport to take the flight to Cairo, arrival and transfer to the hotel, accommodation.
data.mct009.day5.title: DAY 5: WADI RUM – CAIRO
data.mct009.day6.desc: Picnic breakfast, transfer to Cairo airport for the flight to Aswan, arrival and transfer to the boat. Possibility of taking the optional excursion to Abu Simbel by road, return to Aswan and then visit of the Philae Temple (depending on the boat's sailing schedule, these last excursions could be carried out the following day).
data.mct009.day6.title: DAY 6: CAIRO – ASWAN
data.mct009.day7.desc: Full board on board. Sailing towards Kom Ombo, visit the Temple of Kom Ombo, return to the boat and sailing towards Edfu, arrival, dinner and night on board.
data.mct009.day7.title: DAY 7: NILE CRUISE
data.mct009.day8.desc: Full board. At the scheduled time, visit of the Temple of Edfu dedicated to the God Horus. Return to the cruise and sailing towards Esna, after passing through the lock (a construction that allows boats to adapt to a difference in level of 8 metres when passing through a dam), arrival in Luxor, night on board, visit of the great and majestic temples of Karnak and Luxor (depending on sailing schedules, this excursion can be carried out this same afternoon or the following day).
data.mct009.day8.title: DAY 8: NILE CRUISE
data.mct009.day9.desc: Breakfast and check out at 8:00 hrs. Time dedicated to visiting the Colossi of Memnon, the Funerary Temple of Queen Hatshepsut and the Valley of the Kings. At the scheduled time, transfer to Luxor airport bound for Cairo. Upon arrival, reception by our staff and transfer to the hotel in Cairo.
data.mct009.day9.title: DAY 9: LUXOR – CAIRO

data.mct009.overview: Embark on an 11-day journey through the ancient wonders of Egypt and Jordan. Explore the rose-red city of Petra, the vast desert of Wadi Rum, and the historic sites of Madaba and Mount Nebo in Jordan. Then continue to Egypt for a magical Nile cruise between Aswan and Luxor, visiting the iconic Pyramids of Giza, the Valley of the Kings, and the majestic temples of Karnak, Luxor, Edfu, and Kom Ombo.
data.mct009.subtitle: 3 Nights Cairo + 04 Nights Nile Cruise + 01 Night Amman + 02 Nights Petra + 01 Wadi Rum
data.mct009.title: Jewels of Egypt and Jordan 11 Days

data.premium_itinerary_desc: Specially created for lovers of deep archaeology, this premium ten-day itinerary takes you beyond the postcards. Experience all the grandeur of a Nile River cruise, explore mystical temples, and take part in a rare experience: an overnight stay in Abu Simbel with its indescribable night sound and light show, honoring the powerful Ramesses II.

data.trip_to_meknes_exterior_panoramic_v: Trip to Meknes: exterior panoramic view of Bab el Mansour, one of the most spectacular gates in the Arab world. Continue to Fez.
data.tunisia_tour_day1_desc: Assistance and transfer to a 4* Hotel. Accommodation.
data.tunisia_tour_day2_desc: Activities: Departure to the Medina of Tunis (UNESCO World Heritage Site). Walk through the traditional guild-organized souks. Lunch. Departure to Carthage, ancient capital of the Carthaginian empire. Visit the Baths of Antoninus facing the Mediterranean Sea. Continue to the Moorish village of Sidi Bou Said for a walk. Departure to Hammamet. Arrival at 5* Hotel. Dinner and accommodation.
data.tunisia_tour_day2_title: DAY 2: TUNIS - MEDINA - CARTHAGE - SIDI BOU SAID - HAMMAMET
data.tunisia_tour_day3_desc: Activities: Departure to the Ruins of Thugga (modern-day Dougga). Stop in Testour to walk through this medieval village founded by Andalusian Moriscos in the 16th century. Continue to Dougga to visit the most important Roman ruins in Africa (UNESCO World Heritage). Visit the theater, the Church of Victoria, the baths, houses, and temples. Lunch and return to Hammamet. Arrival at 5* Hotel. Dinner and accommodation.
data.tunisia_tour_day4_desc: Activities: Departure to El Jem to visit the Roman amphitheater (3rd largest in the world). Continue to Matmata, the Berber stronghold in the Dahir mountains, famous for its unique underground Troglodyte houses (where Star Wars exterior scenes were filmed). Panoramic stop and lunch in Tamerzet. Continue to Douz, the gateway to the desert. Camel trekking excursion in the dunes. Arrival at 4* Hotel. Dinner and accommodation.
data.tunisia_tour_day4_title: DAY 4: HAMMAMET - EL JEM - MATMATA - DOUZ
data.tunisia_tour_day5_desc: Activities: Cross the Chott el Jerid salt lake by road, witnessing the shifting water colors and mirages. Continue to Tozeur. Arrival and check-in at 4* Hotel. Lunch. In the afternoon, visit the Dar Chraiet Museum and the Date Museum. Dinner and accommodation.
data.tunisia_tour_day5_title: DAY 5: DOUZ - CHOTT EL JERID - TOZEUR
data.tunisia_tour_day6_desc: Activities: Morning visit to the old city and the Souk of Tozeur. Lunch at the hotel. Afternoon at leisure or optional 4x4 excursion to the mountain oases of Chebika, Tamerza, and the desert dunes of Ong Jmel (filming site of The English Patient and Star Wars). Dinner and accommodation at 4* Hotel.
data.tunisia_tour_day7_desc: Activities: Departure to Kairouan. Visit the Great Mosque of Sidi Oqba and the Mausoleum of the Barber (Sidi Sahbi). Lunch. Walk through the ancient medina and departure to Hammamet. Arrival at 5* Hotel. Dinner and accommodation.
data.tunisia_tour_day8_desc: Activities: Free time until the scheduled transfer to Tunis airport for check-in and departure flight.
data.tunisia_tour_excursion_1: 4x4 Mountain Oases & Ong Jmel Excursion (Half day): 60€ per person
data.tunisia_tour_highlight_0: Visit 4 stunning UNESCO World Heritage Sites: Tunis Medina, Carthage, Dougga, and Kairouan.
data.tunisia_tour_highlight_1: Explore legendary cinematic sets from Star Wars in Matmata and Ong Jmel, as well as The English Patient.
data.tunisia_tour_highlight_2: Cross the mystical Chott el Jerid salt lake and enjoy a camel trek into the gateway of the Sahara in Douz.
data.tunisia_tour_highlight_3: Stroll through the iconic, picturesque blue-and-white Moorish village of Sidi Bou Said facing the sea.
data.tunisia_tour_highlight_4: Discover ancient Roman engineering at El Jem, the world's 3rd largest Roman amphitheater.
data.tunisia_tour_hotel_0: Tunis (1 Night): Kyriad Prestige 4* — Bed & Breakfast (AD)

This curated program takes you from the vibrant capital of Tunis to the vast golden dunes of the south, offering a comprehensive and premium travel experience tailored for all groups.

dest.jordan.visaLatinNationalities: Citizens of (Dominican Republic, Colombia, Cuba, and Belize) cannot obtain a visa on arrival. Embassy coordination required in advance, or send data 3 weeks prior at a cost of 40 Jordanian Dinars ($60.00 USD).
dest.jordan.visaPassport: Passport must be valid for at least 6 months beyond entry date and have two blank pages.

faq.aBookingTailor: You can start by visiting our "Tailor a Tour" page where you can choose your desired destinations and specify your travel details. Alternatively, you can contact our luxury travel planners directly through our Contact Us page or via WhatsApp. Once we receive your request, a dedicated concierge will work with you to draft a bespoke itinerary tailored to your preferences.
faq.aBookingVisa: While guests are ultimately responsible for securing their own visas, we provide official booking confirmations and supporting documents required for your visa application. Our concierge team can also offer expert guidance on the visa requirements for Egypt, Jordan, Turkey, and other destinations.
faq.aCancellationsInsurance: Travel insurance is not automatically included in our custom itineraries. We strongly advise all guests to obtain comprehensive travel insurance covering trip cancellation, medical emergencies, evacuation, and baggage loss prior to travel.
faq.aCancellationsPolicy: We understand that plans can change. Cancellations made more than 60 days before the departure date will receive a full refund minus a non-refundable booking fee. Cancellations made between 30 and 60 days before departure are subject to a 50% cancellation fee, while cancellations within 30 days of departure are non-refundable. We highly recommend purchasing comprehensive travel insurance to protect your investment.
faq.aPaymentsMethods: We accept secure online credit card payments (Visa, MasterCard, American Express), bank transfers, and PayPal. For most bespoke bookings, a deposit of 30% is required to secure reservations, with the remaining balance due 30 days prior to your departure date.
faq.aTravelTransport: All our tour packages feature premium private airport transfers. We operate a fleet of luxury sedans, SUVs, and executive coaches. Your professional driver will meet you at the airport arrivals gate with a personalized name board and assist with your luggage, ensuring a seamless and comfortable transition to your hotel.

honeymooners.ctaDesc: Let our expert travel designers craft the perfect romantic getaway tailored just for you.
honeymooners.day10Desc: Breakfast at the hotel. At the scheduled time, transfer to the airport for the return flight.
honeymooners.day1Desc: Arrival at Cairo International Airport. Reception by our representative and private transfer to the hotel. Check-in and accommodation.
honeymooners.day2Desc: After breakfast, depart to visit the famous Pyramids of Giza: Cheops, Chephren, and Mycerinus, as well as the Sphinx. Continue to Saqqara, where we will visit the Step Pyramid of Djoser and Memphis, the ancient capital of Egypt. Return to the hotel and accommodation in Cairo.
honeymooners.day3Desc: Breakfast at the hotel. Visit to the Grand Egyptian Museum (GEM) with its incredible pharaonic collections. Continue to the Citadel of Saladin, the Alabaster Mosque, Coptic Cairo, and the famous Khan El Khalili market. Return to the hotel.
honeymooners.day4Desc: Breakfast. Transfer to the airport for a domestic flight to Luxor. Arrival and embarkation on the Nile Cruise. Visit to the majestic temples of Karnak and Luxor. Dinner and overnight on board.
honeymooners.day5Desc: Visit to the Valley of the Kings, where the tombs of the pharaohs are located, including a visit to the Temple of Queen Hatshepsut and the Colossi of Memnon. Sailing on the Nile towards Edfu. Full board and overnight on board.
honeymooners.day6Desc: Visit to the Temple of Edfu dedicated to the god Horus. Sailing to Kom Ombo and visit to the temple dedicated to the gods Sobek and Horus. Continue sailing to Aswan. Full board and overnight on board.
honeymooners.day7Desc: After breakfast, disembark from the cruise. Panoramic visit of Aswan (according to flight schedule). Transfer to the airport for a flight to Hurghada. Arrival and transfer to the hotel. Accommodation on All Inclusive basis.
honeymooners.day8Desc: Free day to enjoy the Red Sea beaches. Possibility of optional activities such as snorkeling, diving, or boat trips.
honeymooners.day9Desc: Free day to relax and enjoy the hotel, beach, and resort facilities. At the scheduled time, transfer to the airport for a flight to Cairo.
honeymooners.desc: Celebrate your love with an unforgettable honeymoon in Egypt. Romance, history, and luxury await you.
honeymooners.egyptTagline: A perfect trip to celebrate love, combining history, culture, romance, and unforgettable moments on the Red Sea.
honeymooners.heading: Love Stories Begin Here
honeymooners.heroDesc: Let us craft the most romantic chapter of your life. From the timeless pyramids to the tranquil Red Sea, every moment is designed for two.

From the majestic Pyramids of Giza to the tranquil beaches of Hurghada, every moment is designed for romance and discovery. Sail the timeless Nile, explore magnificent temples, and relax in luxury resorts — all crafted to create unforgettable memories for you and your loved one.

mct.estrellas.day10Desc: Morning flight from Amman to Cairo. Afternoon guided visit to the Giza Plateau: the Great Pyramids, the Sphinx, and the Valley Temple. Evening stroll at Khan El Khalili Bazaar. Overnight in Cairo.
mct.estrellas.day11Desc: Morning at the Grand Egyptian Museum (GEM). Afternoon visit to Coptic Cairo: the Hanging Church, the Ben Ezra Synagogue, and the Amr Ibn Al-As Mosque. Overnight in Cairo.
mct.estrellas.day12Desc: Morning flight from Cairo to Aswan. Afternoon visit to the Philae Temple on Agilkia Island and the High Dam. Enjoy a felucca ride at sunset around Elephantine Island and the Botanical Garden. Overnight in Aswan.
mct.estrellas.day13Desc: Early morning drive to Abu Simbel (approx. 3 hours south). Visit the magnificent temples of Ramesses II and Queen Nefertari. Return to Aswan for a free evening. Overnight in Aswan.
mct.estrellas.day14Desc: Check in to your Nile cruise ship. Sail north, stopping at Kom Ombo to visit the double temple dedicated to Sobek and Horus. Continue sailing to Edfu. Overnight on board.
mct.estrellas.day15Desc: Morning visit to the Temple of Horus at Edfu (by horse-drawn carriage). Arrive in Luxor and visit the Karnak Temple complex, the largest religious structure ever built. Evening light show at Karnak (optional). Overnight in Luxor.
mct.estrellas.day16Desc: Full-day exploration of the West Bank: Valley of the Kings, Temple of Hatshepsut at Deir el-Bahari, and the Colossi of Memnon. Afternoon at leisure. Overnight in Luxor.
mct.estrellas.day17Desc: Morning flight from Luxor to Dubai via Cairo. Upon arrival, a guided panoramic tour of the city: Burj Al-Arab, Palm Jumeirah, Atlantis The Palm, and Dubai Marina. Evening at leisure. Overnight in Dubai.
mct.estrellas.day18Desc: Morning free to enjoy the hotel or visit the Dubai Mall. Afternoon at the top of Burj Khalifa – the world’s tallest building. Evening 4×4 desert safari with dune bashing, camel ride, henna, and BBQ dinner under the stars.
mct.estrellas.day19Desc: After breakfast, free time for last shopping or a visit to the Gold Souk. Transfer to Dubai International Airport for your departure flight. End of services.
mct.estrellas.day1Desc: Welcome to İstanbul! Reception at the airport and private transfer to your hotel. The rest of the day is free to relax or begin exploring the city at your own pace. Overnight in İstanbul.
mct.estrellas.day2Desc: After breakfast, embark on a guided tour of the Sultanahmet district: the Hagia Sophia, Blue Mosque, Hippodrome, and Topkapi Palace. In the afternoon, dive into the labyrinthine Grand Bazaar. Evening Bosphorus dinner cruise with traditional music and dancing.
mct.estrellas.day3Desc: Morning Bosphorus cruise along the shores of Europe and Asia. Afternoon visit to the Spice Bazaar and the Dolmabahçe Palace. Free evening to explore the vibrant quarters of Beyoğlu and Taksim.
mct.estrellas.day4Desc: Early morning flight to Cappadocia. Upon arrival, visit the Kaymakli Underground City, the Uchisar Rock Castle, and the Pigeon Valley. Check in to a cave hotel and enjoy a traditional dinner.
mct.estrellas.day5Desc: Optional hot air balloon ride at sunrise. Then explore the Göreme Open-Air Museum, the Pasabag Fairy Chimneys, and the Devrent Valley. Enjoy a pottery workshop in Avanos. Overnight in Cappadocia.
mct.estrellas.day6Desc: Morning flight from Cappadocia to Amman. Afternoon guided visit to the magnificent Roman ruins of Jerash. Transfer to the hotel in Amman for dinner and overnight.
mct.estrellas.day7Desc: Full-day excursion south: visit Madaba to see the famous 6th-century mosaic map of the Holy Land, then Mount Nebo with its panoramic view of the Jordan Valley. Continue to the Dead Sea for a float experience. Return to Amman for overnight.
mct.estrellas.day7Title: Amman – Madaba, Mount Nebo & Dead Sea
mct.estrellas.day8Desc: Drive south to Petra. Enter through the Siq and marvel at the Treasury, the Royal Tombs, the Monastery (optional 800-step climb), and the Colonnaded Street. Dinner and overnight at a hotel near Petra.
mct.estrellas.day9Desc: Morning at leisure in Petra. Afternoon transfer to Wadi Rum. Enjoy a 4×4 jeep tour through the crimson desert and a traditional Bedouin dinner under the stars at a desert camp. Overnight in Wadi Rum.

mct.estrellas.overview: A grand 19-day odyssey spanning four iconic destinations: the mosaic crossroads of İstanbul, the rose-red city of Petra, the timeless pharaonic wonders of Cairo and the Nile, and the dazzling futuristic skyline of Dubai. This carefully curated itinerary blends ancient history, spiritual heritage, desert adventure, and ultra-modern luxury into one seamless, unforgettable journey.

tour_jordan_day10_body: Breakfast and, at the scheduled time, transfer to the airport for the return flight to the country of origin.
tour_jordan_day1_body: Arrival at Cairo Airport. Reception and transfer to the hotel and overnight stay.
tour_jordan_day2_body: Breakfast at the hotel. Departure to the Giza Complex and visit to the three great pyramids: Cheops, Chephren, and Mykerinus. Visit to the Valley Temple of King Chephren and the Sphinx. Lunch at a local restaurant. Visit to the Grand Egyptian Museum (GEM), the largest museum dedicated to Ancient Egypt, housing around 100,000 artifacts, including the complete collection of Tutankhamun, exhibited for the first time in a single location. Return to the hotel and overnight stay in Cairo. Note: Entry into the interior of the Great Pyramid requires an extra ticket, purchased locally, in limited numbers, and is not included.
tour_jordan_day2_title: Cairo / Pyramids of Giza / Sphinx / Grand Egyptian Museum (GEM)
tour_jordan_day3_body: Breakfast and departure in the morning by tourist bus towards Tell Basta (located in the city of Zagazig). We will visit the ruins of one of the Egyptian temples and the well from which the Holy Family fetched water. We will continue to Tanis, a city that was for a period the capital of Egypt during the New Kingdom and, according to some Egyptologists, corresponds to ancient Pi-Ramesses. Then, continuation to Ismailia for lunch and a panoramic tour of the city, known as the greenest in Egypt. Return to the hotel in Cairo and overnight stay.
tour_jordan_day3_title: Cairo / Tell Basta / Tanis / Ismailia / Cairo
tour_jordan_day4_body: Breakfast and departure in the morning to the neighborhood of El Matarya, where we will visit the Church of the Virgin and the tree whose shade protected the Holy Family. We will continue to Mostorod to visit the Church of the Virgin. Afterwards, transfer to the city of Belbeis, 55 km from Cairo, to visit the Church of the Virgin Mary. Lunch at a local restaurant. Return to Cairo. Free afternoon. Overnight stay in Cairo.
tour_jordan_day5_body: Breakfast and departure in the morning to Wadi El Natrun, located 120 km from the capital. We will visit three important Coptic monasteries: Deir Al Baramos, Deir Anba Bechoy, and Deir Santa Maria. Lunch at a local restaurant. Return to Cairo. Free afternoon. Overnight stay in Cairo.
tour_jordan_day6_body: Breakfast. Visit to the Coptic Quarter, including the churches of Saint Sergius, Abu Seifein, and Saint Barbara. Lunch. In the afternoon, departure to the neighborhood of El Maadi, south of the city, to see the Church of Al Adra Al Adawia. Return to the hotel in Cairo and overnight stay.
tour_jordan_day7_body: Breakfast and departure towards the south of Egypt, to El Minya (250 km from the capital). Visit to the Monastery of the Virgin, situated on Mount Al Tair. Lunch and dinner at the hotel. Accommodation and overnight stay in El Minya.
tour_jordan_day8_body: Breakfast. Departure to Asiut, the provincial capital, 120 km south of El Minya. We will visit the Monastery of Al Moharrak, located on Mount Qusqam. Lunch and dinner at the hotel. Overnight stay in El Minya.
tour_jordan_day9_body: Breakfast and departure to Cairo via another route, taking advantage of the journey to visit the Monastery of the Holy Family on Mount Dronka. Picnic lunch. Arrival in Cairo. Overnight stay.
tour_jordan_excludes_1: Entry visa to Egypt: US$ 30 dollars per person.
tour_jordan_excludes_2: Travel and assistance expenses: US$ 45 dollars per adult or child, mandatory payment at destination (does not include tips for the guide, left to the client's discretion regarding their satisfaction with the services provided).
tour_jordan_highlight_1: Giza Pyramids, Sphinx and the Grand Egyptian Museum (GEM).
tour_jordan_highlight_2: Spiritual pilgrimage along the Coptic Route and Holy Family well.
tour_jordan_highlight_3: Visits to historical desert monasteries of Wadi El Natrun, Asiut, and Mount Al Tair.
tour_jordan_highlight_4: The Cave Church (Monastery of Saint Simon) in the Mokattam Mountain.
tour_jordan_highlight_5: Famous Byzantine mosaic map of Palestine in Madaba and Mount Nebo.
tour_jordan_highlight_6: Full-day private tour of the Rose-Red City of Petra.
tour_jordan_highlight_7: Stargazing and 2-hour 4x4 Jeep tour in the red sands of Wadi Rum.
tour_jordan_hotel_1: Cairo: Barcelo Pyramids, Cairo Pyramids or similar.
tour_jordan_hotel_2: El Minya: Grand Aton Minya or similar.
tour_jordan_includes_1: 2 nights in El Minya on a half-board basis (breakfast and dinner).
tour_jordan_includes_2: 7 nights in Cairo on a bed and breakfast accommodation basis.
tour_jordan_includes_3: All visits, entrance fees, and transfers according to the itinerary.
tour_jordan_includes_6: All transfers by land in modern, air-conditioned vehicles.
tour_jordan_itinerary_title: Itinerary: Journey of the Holy Family – 10 Days – 09 Nights
tour_jordan_overview_1: Embark on an extraordinary 14-day combined tour spanning Egypt and Jordan. This carefully crafted itinerary takes you on a spiritual and historical pilgrimage, tracing the footsteps of the Holy Family through Egypt's ancient Coptic monasteries, and exploring the dramatic rose-red valleys of Petra and Wadi Rum in Jordan.
tour_jordan_overview_2: Beginning in Cairo, you will witness the majestic Giza Pyramids and the newly opened Grand Egyptian Museum. You will then trace Coptic routes through historic sites like Tell Basta, Wadi El Natrun, and the Coptic Quarter, before driving south to El Minya and Asiut to explore sacred mountaintop monasteries.
tour_jordan_overview_3: The second half of your journey takes you to Jordan, starting from Madaba's famous mosaic map and Mount Nebo to the legendary city of Petra and the Martian landscapes of Wadi Rum, finishing with premium services and absolute comfort throughout.
tour_jordan_price_double: Value per person in double or triple: 944 USD.
tour_jordan_title: JOURNEY OF THE HOLY FAMILY – 10 DAYS – 09 NIGHTS
tour_jordan_visited_1: Cairo: visit to the Pyramids complex, Valley Temple and Sphinx, Grand Egyptian Museum (GEM).
tour_jordan_visited_2: Tell Basta / Ismailia / Wadi El Natrun.
tour_jordan_visited_3: El Minya: Al Ashmonien, Beni Hassan, Tell El Amarna, and Tuna El Gebel.
tour_siwa_day1_body: After breakfast at the hotel, early departure towards the Wadi El Natroun Valley, named after the 8 different lakes in the region where natron salt is produced. Visit to the Monastery of Anba Bishoy (116KM), the most famous monastery of the Coptic Orthodox Church of Alexandria. Continue to El Alamein (176KM) on the shores of the Mediterranean, stop for lunch at a local restaurant (excluding drinks). In the afternoon, we will visit the WWII Museum and the Commonwealth Tombs, continuing along the Mediterranean coast to Marsa Matrouh (184KM). Arrival at the Marsa Matrouh Hotel. Dinner (excluding drinks) and overnight stay.
tour_siwa_day2_body: After breakfast, check out from the hotel and visit Rommel's Cave, featuring rock paintings dating back more than 5,000 years. Continue to Siwa Oasis (307KM) crossing the Sahara Desert. Arrival and accommodation at the Siwa Shali Resort Hotel, where we will have some time to rest. Stop for lunch at a local restaurant (excluding drinks). Late afternoon, a walk through the Shali Old Village to contemplate the sunset. Return to the hotel, dinner (excluding drinks), and overnight stay.
tour_siwa_day3_body: After breakfast, we will visit the Mountain of the Dead, the Temple of Amun, and Cleopatra's Baths. Stop for lunch (excluding drinks). In the afternoon, we will head out in a 4x4 vehicle to the Great Sand Sea, arriving at the desert tents. Dinner (excluding drinks) and overnight stay in the camp.
tour_siwa_day4_body: After breakfast at the hotel, departure for Alexandria (598km). Stop in Marsa Matrouh for lunch at a local restaurant (excluding drinks). Afterwards, continue to Alexandria and check into the hotel. Dinner included (excluding drinks) and overnight stay.
tour_siwa_day5_body: After breakfast at the hotel, we will visit the ancient capital of Egypt for over 1,000 years, famous for the Lighthouse of Alexandria (one of the 7 wonders of the ancient world). We will visit the Kom El Shoqafa catacombs, Pompey's Pillar (a monument carved from a single piece of red granite weighing 396 tons), Montazah Palace, and the Qaitbay Citadel, built on the exact site of the former lighthouse. We will also visit the famous Library of Alexandria (closed on Fridays), one of the largest and most important in the world. Stop for an included lunch at a local restaurant (excluding drinks). Next, we return to Cairo (218 km) directly to the airport or your chosen hotel. End of services.
tour_siwa_includes_1: Round-trip transportation by bus or minibus Cairo – Siwa – Cairo
tour_siwa_includes_2: 01 night in Marsa Matruh with breakfast and dinner (w/o drinks)
tour_siwa_includes_3: 03 nights in Siwa with full board (w/o drinks)
tour_siwa_includes_4: 01 night in a camp in Alexandria with breakfast and dinner (w/o drinks)
tour_siwa_includes_5: Tour to Saint Bishoy Monastery and WWII Museum
tour_siwa_includes_6: Tour to Mountain of the Dead, Temple of Amun, Cleopatra's Bath, Great Sand Sea, Gebal Dakror, and Fatnas Island sunset
tour_siwa_includes_8: Accompaniment by a team representative throughout the trip
tour_siwa_summary: Cairo → Wadi El Natroun → Marsa Matruh → Siwa → Alexandria → Cairo
